example.vi: Anh ấy là giáo viên

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE SENTENCE STRUCTURE
sections.explanation: In this free grammar lesson, we will teach you the most basic Vietnamese sentence structures. You will be able to use them immediately in your daily life. Let’s check it out!.

sections.heading: SUBJECT + NOUN
sections.explanation: To give an introduction about a person or a thing, make a statement, use the following sentence structure: Vietnamese language functions as am/is/are in English.

examples.vi: Tôi là David I am David Anh ấy là giáo viên He is a
Cô ấy là bác sĩ She is a
Chúng tôi là người Việt Nam We are Vietnamese Họ là người Mỹ They are American Bánh mì là đồ ăn Việt Nam Banh mi is a Vietnamese food

sections.heading: SUBJECT+ ADJECTIVES
sections.explanation: In the Vietnamese language, to-be verbs are not placed before an adjective as it does in English. You just need to add the adjective behind a subject.

examples.vi: Tôi đẹp I am
ấy xấu He is ugly Chị ấy thông minh She is
Chúng tôi ngu ngốc We are
Họ thân thiện They are
Bánh mì ngon Banh mi is

sections.heading: SUBJECT + VERB
sections.explanation: To indicate or state an action, you can use the sentence structure below:

examples.vi: Tôi ăn I
Tôi đã ăn I ate Anh ấy uống He
Chị ấy đang ngủ She is
Chúng tôi sẽ học We will

sections.heading: SUBJECT + VERB + OBJECT
sections.explanation: To make a sentence more detailed and informative, you can add an object to the structure the same way as in English.

examples.vi: Tôi ăn cơm I eat rice Anh ấy uống nước He
Chị ấy học Tiếng Việt She
Chúng tôi đọc sách We read a book Họ nghe nhạc They
mèo bắt con chuột A cat

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese a SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object.

example.vi: Tôi ăn cơm mỗi ngày

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE PRESENT TENSE
sections.explanation: Unlike English, the Vietnamese language doesn’t have specific verb conjugations to indicate tenses. Instead, time adverbs are used to indicate when the action is occurring. We teach you a few time adverbs and the marker “đang”.

sections.heading: AFFIRMATIVE – PRESENT SIMPLE
sections.explanation: Present simple tense is considered the default tense in Vietnamese.

examples.vi: ấy đi ngủ lúc 10 giờ tối He goes to bed at 10 pm Cô ấy làm việc hôm nay She goes to work
Chị ấy học bài mỗi tháng She

sections.heading: Using đang
sections.explanation: To express that the action is occurring right now, you can use “đang” which means “being/be doing” :

examples.vi: Bây giờ , tôi đang ăn cơm I am
Hiện tại , tôi đang uống nước At
examples.en: presen t, I am drinking water Anh

examples.vi: ấy đang ngủ ngay lúc này He is
Cô ấy đang làm việc bây giờ She is
Chị ấy đang học bài ngay lúc này She is
ấy đang không ngủ He is not
Cô ấy không làm việc hôm nay She
Chị ấy không học bài She

faqs.question: How to use the Vietnamese present tense?
faqs.answer: Present simple tense is considered the default tense in Vietnamese.

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. — Learn more about the Vietnamese Sentence Structure here.

items.title: Past simple tense in Vietnamese
items.pattern: Subject + đã + verb + (past tense time adverb)

example.vi: Tôi đã đi học hôm qua

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE PAST SIMPLE TENSE
sections.explanation: Unlike English, there are not any specific verb conjugations to indicate tenses in Vietnamese. Instead, there are time adverbs to indicate when the action is occurring. Today, we’ll learn a few adverbs and two markers “đã”and “rồi” to express past actions in Vietnamese.

examples.vi: “đã”and “rồi” to

sections.heading: AFFIRMATIVE – PAST SIMPLE
sections.explanation: In order to indicate that an action occurred in the past, “đã” or “rồi” (both mean as “done”) are used with the verb as following below pattern: PATTERN #1 Subject + đã + verb + (past tense time adverb) PATTERN #2 Subject + verb + rồi (already) + (past tense time adverb) Here are few common time adverbs which are used to describe a past action: Hôm qua Yesterday Tuần trước Last week Tháng trước Last month Năm ngoái Last year For.

examples.vi: Tôi đã đi học hôm qua I did go to
Chị ấy đã đi du lịch Hàn Quốc năm ngoái She did
examples.en: travel to Korea last year Anh

examples.vi: ấy ăn cơm rồi He did eat rice
Tháng trước, chúng tôi cưới nhau rồi Last
Mình đã học bài tuần trước I did

sections.heading: NEGATIVE – PAST SIMPLE TENSE
sections.pattern: Subject + chưa (not yet) + Verb + (past time adverb)

examples.vi: ấy đã không ăn cơm He did not eat rice yet Tháng trước, chúng tôi chưa cưới nhau Last
examples.en: month, we did not marry yet

examples.vi: Mình chưa học bài tuần trước I did not

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese SOV or SVO?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. — Learn more about the Vietnamese Sentence Structure here.

extracted_patterns: Subject + đã + không (not) + Verb + (past time adverb)
Subject + chưa (not yet) + Verb + (past time adverb)

items.title: Future tense in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi sẽ ăn cơm ngày mai.

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE FUTURE TENSE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing the future tense is simple and primarily done using the word “sẽ” before the verb. We teach you how to form future tense sentences, providing and tips for talking about future plans or events in everyday conversation.

sections.heading: WILL + VERB
sections.explanation: When you want to express the action which will occur, please use “sẽ” which means “will” before verb:

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ ăn cơm ngày mai I will have rice
Chị ấy sẽ học bài 5 ngày sau She will
Chúng tôi sẽ đi Hà Nội tuần sau We will go to Ha Noi in next week Họ sẽ đi làm tháng sau They will go to work in next
Năm sau, tôi sẽ lấy vợ Next
Tôi sắp ăn cơm bây giờ I will soon have rice now Chị ấy sắp học bài một tí nữa She will soon
Chúng tôi sắp đi Hà Nội bây giờ We will soon go to Ha Noi now Họ sắp đi làm một tí nữa They will soon go to work a
Một tí nữa, tôi sắp lấy vợ A
examples.en: little bit later, I will soon marry

sections.heading: NEGATIVE FORM
sections.explanation: In future tense, you just need to add “sẽ không” (means “will not”) before verb to make a negative sentence: For.
sections.pattern: Subject + sẽ không + Verb + (future time adverb)

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ không ăn cơm ngày mai I will not have rice
Chị ấy sẽ không học bài 5 ngày sau She will not
Chúng tôi sẽ không đi Hà Nội tuần sau We will not go to Ha Noi in next week Họ sẽ không đi làm tháng sau They will not go to work in next
Năm sau, tôi sẽ không lấy vợ Next
examples.en: year, I will not marry NOTE || Review the Vietnamese negative form in this free lesson.

sections.heading: INTERROGATIVE
sections.explanation: In order to turn the affirmative sentence into an interrogative one, you just need to add the question words to the future tense sentence. Here are of questions in future tense :

examples.vi: Bạn sẽ đi học ngày mai không? Will you go to
Bạn sẽ làm gì tuần sau? What will you do next
sẽ đi đâu tháng sau? Where will you go next
sẽ ăn cơm với tôi? Who will eat rice with me? Khi nào bạn sẽ đi Mỹ? When will you go to America? Vì sao bạn sẽ nghỉ việc? Why will you

faqs.question: How to form the Vietnamese future tense?
faqs.answer: The future tense is formed by adding “sẽ” before the main verb in a sentence. For example, “Tôi sẽ đi” means “I will go.”

faqs.question: Can “sẽ” be used for all types of future actions?
faqs.answer: Yes, “sẽ” can be used for any future actions or events, whether they are certain or just planned.

faqs.question: Do Vietnamese future tense sentences need time adverbs?
faqs.answer: While time expressions like “tomorrow” ( ngày mai ) or “next week” ( tuần sau ) can clarify when something will happen, they are not necessary if the context is clear.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “sắp” and “sẽ”?
faqs.answer: “Sắp” indicates that something is going to happen very soon, implying immediacy, while “sẽ” is used for future actions in general, without specifying when they will occur. For example, “Tôi sắp đi” means “I am about to go,” whereas “Tôi sẽ đi” means “I will go.”

faqs.question: Can “sắp” and “sẽ” be used together?
faqs.answer: No, “sắp” and “sẽ” are not used together because they serve different purposes. “Sắp” emphasizes an action that is about to happen, while “sẽ” is used for general future actions. For example, you would say “Tôi sắp đến” (I am about to arrive) or “Tôi sẽ đến” (I will arrive), but not both.

extracted_patterns: Subject + sẽ không + Verb + (future time adverb)

example.vi: Tôi đang ăn cơm bây giờ

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE CONTINUOUS TENSE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, the continuous tense is formed by using the word “đang” before the main verb to indicate an ongoing action. This lesson will explain how to construct continuous tense sentences and highlight when to use this form in everyday conversations.

sections.explanation: In continuous tense, you just need to add “không” (means “not”) before verb to make a negative sentence:

examples.vi: Tôi không đang ăn cơm bây giờ I ‘m not

faqs.question: How to form the continuous tense?
faqs.answer: The continuous tense is formed by adding “đang” before the main verb. For example, “Tôi đang ăn” means “I am eating.”

faqs.question: Can “đang” be used for both present and future continuous actions?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đang” is used to indicate ongoing actions in the present. To refer to future ongoing actions, a future marker like “sẽ” can be combined with “đang” (e.g., “Tôi sẽ đang làm việc” – “I will be working”).

faqs.question: Can “đang” be used for past continuous actions?
faqs.answer: Yes, to express past continuous, combine “đang” with a time reference for the past (e.g., “Hôm qua, tôi đang đọc sách” – “Yesterday, I was reading”).

faqs.question: Is “đang” always required to express continuous actions in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, to explicitly express that an action is ongoing, “đang” is required. However, in casual speech, context might make the use of “đang” optional.

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE PLURALS
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, forming plurals is not as straightforward as in other languages since nouns do not change form. Instead, words like những and các are used to indicate plurality, along with context to understand whether a noun is singular or plural.

examples.vi: những and các are used to
examples.en: indicate plurality, along with context to understand whether a noun is singular or plural.

sections.heading: GENERAL PLURALS
sections.explanation: To make words plurals simply add “những” or “các” next to the counter of a singular noun, then the word will become the plural: • Các is used when referring to the entire group or all members of a certain category. Here is a reminder of the most common counters in Vietnamese: Counter Used for Cái General non-living things Con Animal Cây Pen/Tree/Pencil Tờ Paper/ Newspaper Cuốn Book/ Notebook Bức Letter/Picture/Photo Chiếc Car/Phone/Bike Tấm Cloth/ Photo Viên Ice/ Marble/ Meatball Chai Bottle Ly Glass Lon Can For.

Những con mèo → Những con mèo đang ăn những con cá Cats → The cats are
Các tấm ảnh → Các tấm ảnh cũ kỹ Photos → The
Các viên đá → Các viên đá hình con cá Ice
examples.en: cubes → The ice cubes are all shaped as fishes

examples.vi: Những cây bút chì → Những cây bút chì màu đen Pencils → The
Những tờ báo → Những tờ báo đang bay Newspapers → The
Những cuốn sách → Những cuốn sách của ba tôi rất hay Books → The
examples.en: books of my dad are good

examples.en: glasses of wine of my mom are all finished

examples.vi: Các lon bia → Các lon bia đã được ướp lạnh Cans of beer → The cans of beer are

sections.heading: FOR PEOPLE
sections.explanation: When it concerns people, counters are not used to make the words become plural. Just simply add “những/các” to the singular noun only.

examples.vi: Những cô gái → Những cô gái đang nói chuyện Girls → The
Các ca sỹ → Các ca sỹ đang hát Singers → The
Những chàng trai → Những chàng trai đang hát Boys → The boys are

Các bác sĩ → Các bác sĩ đang cười Doctors → The
Các ba mẹ → Các ba mẹ đang ngủ Parents → The

faqs.question: How do you form plurals in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Plurals in Vietnamese are often formed by adding những or các before the noun. For example, “những chiếc xe” (some cars) or “các con mèo” (all the cats).

faqs.question: What is the difference between những and các ?
faqs.answer: Both những and các are used to indicate plurals, but they have slightly different nuances: • Những is commonly used for plural nouns when the context does not imply totality . • Các is used when referring to the entire group or all members of a certain category.

faqs.question: Can nouns be plural without những or các ?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vietnamese nouns can often be plural based on context alone, without needing những or các . For instance, “Tôi thích mèo” can mean “I like cats” without explicitly marking the plural.

faqs.question: Are there plural forms for pronouns?
faqs.answer: Yes, personal pronouns in Vietnamese can be made plural by adding chúng or các before the pronoun. For example, “chúng tôi” (we), “các bạn” (you all).

example.vi: Anh ấy không ăn kem

sections.heading: USING THE NEGATION IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: We have learned about the basic Vietnamese sentence structure. We now also need to understand how to negate a sentence to use in simple conversations. In this free lesson we teach you the most common ways to make negative sentences in Vietnamese language.

sections.heading: CASE 1: KHÔNG / KHÔNG PHẢI LÀ (NOT)
sections.explanation: This is one of the easiest ways to make a negative sentence in Vietnamese. You only need to add “KHÔNG” in front of the verb or adjective, or “KHÔNG PHẢI LÀ” in front of the noun. PATTERN #1 không + Verb / Adjective PATTERN #2 không phải là + Noun For.

examples.vi: Tôi không thích mưa I
ấy không ăn kem He
Chị ấy không có tiền She
Chúng tôi không nghèo We are not poor Họ không thông minh They are not
Các chị ấy không xấu Those
tôi không phải là bác sĩ My
Phở không phải là món ăn Ý Pho is not an Italian food Hiragana không phải là bảng chữ cái Tiếng Việt Hiragana is not a Vietnamese

sections.heading: CASE 2: CHƯA / CHƯA PHẢI LÀ (NOT YET)
sections.explanation: To express “not yet” you only need to add “CHƯA” in front of the verb or adjective, or “CHƯA PHẢI LÀ” in front of the noun. PATTERN #1 chưa + Verb / Adjective PATTERN #2 chưa phải là + Noun For.

examples.vi: Tôi chưa ăn cơm I
ấy chưa tắm He
Cô ấy chưa làm bài tập She
Chúng tôi chưa đói We are not
Họ chưa no They are not full yet Các ông chưa khoẻ The old men did not feel good yet Tháng 3 chưa phải là mùa mưa ở Sài Gòn March is not a

examples.vi: chưa phải là chồng tôi David is not my
Thứ 5 chưa phải là ngày cuối tuần Thursday is not the

sections.heading: CASE 3: KHÔNG THỂ/ KHÔNG THỂ LÀ (CANNOT)
sections.explanation: To express “can’t” you only need to add “KHÔNG THỂ” in front of the verb or adjective, “KHÔNG THỂ LÀ” in front of the noun. PATTERN #1 không thể + Verb / Adjective PATTERN #2 không thể là + Noun For.

faqs.question: How to express “not” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: This is one of the easiest ways to make a negative sentence in Vietnamese. You only need to add “KHÔNG” in front of the verb or adjective, or “KHÔNG PHẢI LÀ” in front of the noun. #1 không + Verb / Adjective #2 không phải là + Noun For example: Tôi không thích mưa. I don’t like rain Anh ấy không ăn kem. He doesn’t eat ice cream Chị ấy không có tiền.

faqs.question: She doesn’t have money How to express “not yet” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express “not yet” you only need to add “CHƯA” in front of the verb or adjective, or “CHƯA PHẢI LÀ” in front of the noun. #1 chưa + Verb / Adjective #2 chưa phải là + Noun For example: Tôi chưa ăn cơm. I haven’t eaten yet Anh ấy chưa tắm. He hasn’t showered yet Cô ấy chưa làm bài tập.

faqs.question: She hasn’t done homework yet How to express “cannot” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express “can’t” you only need to add “KHÔNG THỂ” in front of the verb or adjective, “KHÔNG THỂ LÀ” in front of the noun. #1 không thể + Verb / Adjective #2 không thể là + Noun For example: Tôi không thể bơi. I cannot swim Anh ấy không thể nói. He cannot speak Cô ấy không thể nghe.

faqs.question: She cannot hear How to express “not allowed ” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express not being allowed to do something, add “KHÔNG ĐƯỢC” in front of the verb only . không được + Verb For example: Tôi không được ngủ quá 8 tiếng. I am not allowed to sleep over 8 hours Anh ấy không được cười lớn ở bệnh viện. He is not allowed to laugh at hospital Chị ấy không được nói. She is not allowed to talk

sections.heading: HOW TO USE VIETNAMESE ADJECTIVES
sections.explanation: Vietnamese adjectives are pretty easy to use once you understand the sentence structures required to form sentences. We teach you 3 simple sentence structures to start using Vietnamese adjectives immediately.

sections.heading: SUBJECT + ADJECTIVE
sections.explanation: You can add one or more adjectives behind the subject to make a statement about a person, an animal, a thing, or an event. For.

examples.vi: Tôi đẹp I am

faqs.question: How to use adjectives with a subject?
faqs.answer: You can add one or more adjectives behind the subject to make a statement about a person, an animal, a thing, or an event. Subject + Adjective NOTE || Add “và” between 2 or more adjectives For example: Tôi đẹp . I am pretty Anh ấy dễ thương . He is cute Trời nóng và nắng .

faqs.question: The weather is hot and sunny How to use adjectives with a noun?
faqs.answer: Add one or more than one adjective behind a noun t o indicate more details about an object in an SVO sentence. Subject + Adjective For example: Tôi ăn tô phở nóng và rẻ . I eat a hot and cheap Pho Anh ấy thích matcha lạnh và ngọt . He likes a cold and sweet matcha Chị ấy không thích đầm dài và sặc sỡ .

How to use adverbs in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can add an adverb behind the verb to indicate a state or a level of an action . Verb + Adverb For example: Tôi hát hay . I sing well Anh ấy nấu ăn vào buổi sáng . He cooks in the morning. — You can add an adverb behind or in front the adjective to indicate a level of characteristics. Adjective + Adverb Đẹp hú hồn ! Amazingly beautiful! Lạnh tê tái ! Frozenly cold! — Learn more about Vietnamese adverbs here.

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE PREPOSITIONS
sections.explanation: Prepositions play a crucial role in Vietnamese grammar, as they help express relationships between nouns and other words in a sentence. This lesson will explore common Vietnamese prepositions, their meanings, and how to use them correctly in various contexts.

sections.heading: PARTICLE Ở
sections.explanation: There is only one preposition particle in Vietnamese which is “ở”. You can use this particle to describe the position of things following this.

faqs.question: What are the most common prepositions?
faqs.answer: Some common Vietnamese prepositions include trên (on), dưới (under), trước (in front of), sau (behind), and giữa (between).

faqs.question: How do prepositions function in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Prepositions in Vietnamese typically indicate relationships of place, time, and direction, often appearing before a noun or pronoun to provide context, such as “cái bàn ở trên” (the table is on top).

faqs.question: Do prepositions change based on gender?
faqs.answer: No, Vietnamese prepositions do not change based on gender or number; they remain the same regardless of the nouns they refer to.

faqs.question: How to express time using prepositions?
faqs.answer: Time expressions often use prepositions such as trong (in) for durations, like “trong một giờ” (in an hour), or trước (before) and sau (after) for specific times, like “trước 5 giờ” (before 5 o’clock).

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE QUESTIONS
sections.explanation: In this lesson, we will help you practice asking questions in Vietnamese with: WH-question words and “Yes or No” questions. Let’s start!.

sections.heading: QUESTION WORDS || WHAT, WHO, WHERE
sections.explanation: Here are Vietnamese question words, let’s start with What – Who – Where : What Who Where …gì? …ai? Ai…? …đâu? Clause + gì? Clause + ai? / Ai + clause? Clause + đâu? Hôm nay ăn gì ? Bạn là ai ? / Ai là bạn? Bạn đi đâu ? What did you eat today? Who are you? Where do you go? NOTE // “ai” is a question word that can be placed either at the beginning or at the end of the question. There are few common prepositions that you can use with “đâu” such as: ở đâu? = in where/ at where? ; đến đâu? = to where? More here: for ”What”: Cái gì ?!!! What?!! Đây là gì ? What is it? Em đang học gì ? What are you studying? Bạn làm nghề gì ? What do you do? (occupation) Anh đang làm gì ? What are you doing? for ”Who”: Ai ?!!! Who?!! Đây là ai ? Ai đây? Who is it? Cô giáo em là ai ? Ai là cô giáo em? Who is your teacher? Bạn làm việc với ai ? Ai làm việc với bạn? What do you work with? Ai là idol của bạn? Idol của bạn là ai ? Who is your idol? for ”Where”: Ở đâu ?!!! Where?!! Đây là đâu ? Where is it? Em đang ở đâu ? Where are you? Bạn đi đến đâu ? Where do you go? Anh ngồi đâu ? Where are you sitting?.

sections.heading: HOW TO COUNT IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: It’s important to learn how to count in Vietnamese. In this lesson, we will help you to master counting from 1 to 1 million in this language in a few minutes!.

sections.heading: COUNTING FROM 1 TO 10
sections.explanation: For now, let’s brush up counting from 1 to 10 in Vietnamese: Number Reading 0 Lẻ 1 Một 2 Hai 3 Ba 4 Bốn 5 Năm 6 Sáu 7 Bảy 8 Tám 9 Chín 10 Mười.

examples.vi: Lẻ 1 Một 2 Hai 3

sections.heading: COUNTING FROM 11 TO 19
sections.explanation: After mastering counting from 1 to 10, let’s read numbers from 11 to 19 by following this rule: 10 (Mười – Ten) + 1 (Một – One) = Mười một (Eleven) Number Reading Number Reading 11 Mười một 16 Mười sáu 12 Mười hai 17 Mười bảy 13 Mười ba 18 Mười tám 14 Mười bốn 19 Mười chín 15 Mười lăm.

sections.heading: COUNTING FROM 20 TO 99
sections.explanation: First, let’s learn how to read 20-30-40-50-60-70-80-90: Number Reading Number Reading 20 Hai Mươi 60 Sáu Mươi 30 Ba Mươi 70 Bảy Mươi 40 Bốn Mươi 80 Tám Mươi 50 Năm Mươi 90 Chín Mươi Then, we apply the same rule of composing to read numbers from 20 to 99. NOTE • When the digit “2-3-4-5-6-7-8-9” is followed by the digit “1”, we don’t read “1” as “một” but we read “mốt” • When the digit “1-2-3-4-5-6-7-8-9” is followed by the digit “5”, we don’t read “5” as “năm” but we read “lăm” For.

Chổi: 1 cây Broom: 1 Có 3 cây chổi There are 3
Báo: 5 tờ Newspaper: 5 Có 5 tờ báo There are 5
Khăn giấy: 6 tờ Tissue

sections.heading: MORE COUNTERS
sections.explanation: In addition, here is a table of other 8 counters in Vietnamese: Counter Used for Cuốn Book / Notebook 1 cuốn, 2 cuốn, 3 cuốn Bức Letter / Picture / Photo 1 bức, 2 bức, 3 bức Chiếc Car / Phone / Bike 1 chiếc, 2 chiếc, 3 chiếc Tấm Cloth / Photo 1 tấm, 2 tấm, 3 tấm Viên Ice / Marble / Meatball 1 viên, 2 viên, 3 viên Chai Bottle 1 chai, 2 chai, 3 chai Ly Glass 1 ly, 2 ly, 3 ly Lon Can 1 lon, 2 lon, 3 lon.

examples.en: chai, 2 chai, 3 chai Ly Glass 1 ly, 2 ly, 3 ly Lon Can 1 lon, 2 lon, 3 lon

faqs.question: What are measure words?
faqs.answer: The concept of measuring words actually exists in other languages! Like in English for certain nouns such as three glasses, or a bar of chocolate for example. The only difference in Vietnamese is that all nouns must have a measure word before them.

faqs.question: What is the Vietnamese counter for people?
faqs.answer: You will use người to count person/people: Number + người 15 người . 15 people Gia đình tôi có 4 người .

faqs.question: My family has 4 people What is the Vietnamese counter for animals?
faqs.answer: You will use con to count animals: Number + con + animal Cá: 15 con . Fish: 15 units Có 15 con cá.

faqs.question: There are 15 fishes What is the Vietnamese counter for books?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese counter for books is Cuốn. For example: 1 cuốn, 2 cuốn, 3 cuốn (1 book, 2 books, 3 books)

extracted_patterns: Number + cái + non-living thing Bàn: 5 cái Table: 5 units Có 5 cái bàn
Number + con + animal Cá: 15 con Fish: 15 units Có 15 con cá
Number + người 15 người 15 people Gia đình tôi có 4 người My family has 4 people

items.title: Writing dates in Vietnamese

example.vi: Thứ hai, Ngày 23 Tháng 02 Năm 2024

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE DATES
sections.explanation: Knowing how to say years, months, and days will allow you to talk about your past activities, future plans and so much more! Learn how to write the date in Vietnamese and how to ask for it.

sections.heading: DAYS OF THE WEEK
sections.explanation: Before anything else, let’s learn the days of the week in Vietnamese: English Vietnamese Monday Thứ hai Tuesday Thứ ba Wednesday Thứ tư Thursday Thứ năm Friday Thứ sáu Saturday Thứ bảy Sunday Chủ Nhật From “Monday ” to “Saturday”, you only need to memorise the structure: Thứ + number in order from 2 to 7 Except for Sunday which is “Chủ Nhật” in Vietnamese.

examples.vi: Thứ hai Tuesday Thứ ba Wednesday Thứ tư Thursday Thứ năm Friday Thứ sáu Saturday Thứ bảy Sunday Chủ Nhật From

sections.heading: DAYS OF THE MONTH
sections.explanation: Next, in order to express the day of the month, you just use this structure: 1 2 3 4 5 6 Một Hai Ba Bốn Năm Sáu 7 8 9 10 11 12 Bảy Tám Chín Mười Mười một Mười hai 13 14 15 16 17 18 Mười ba Mười bốn Mười lăm Mười sáu Mười bảy Mười tám 19 20 21 22 23 24 Mười chín Hai mươi Hai mươi mốt Hai mươi hai Hai mươi ba Hai mươi bốn 25 26 27 28 29 30 Hai mươi lăm Hai mươi sáu Hai mươi bảy Hai mươi tám Hai mươi chín Ba mươi 31 Ba mươi mốt.

sections.heading: MONTHS IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Now, let’s learn about 12 months in Vietnamese with this structure: 1 2 3 4 5 6 Một Hai Ba Bốn Năm Sáu 7 8 9 10 11 12 Bảy Tám Chín Mười Mười một Mười hai.

sections.heading: YEAR IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: For years , you can use this structure: 1 to 2000: Number Reading Number Reading Number Reading Number Reading 1000 Một nghìn 100 Một trăm 10 Mười 1 Một 2000 Hai nghìn 200 Hai trăm 20 Hai mươi 2 Hai 300 Ba trăm 30 Ba mươi 3 Ba 400 Bốn trăm 40 Bốn mươi 4 Bốn 500 Năm trăm 50 Năm mươi 5 Năm 600 Sáu trăm 60 Sáu mươi 6 Sáu 700 Bảy trăm 70 Bảy mươi 7 Bảy 800 Tám trăm 80 Tám mươi 8 Tám 900 Chín trăm 90 Chín mươi 9 Chín For.
sections.pattern: Năm + number Năm: Year Let’s brush up again this table counting from

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE DATE FORMAT
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, the date format consists of 4 elements in this order structure: PATTERN Week day + date + month + year Thứ …, Ngày … Tháng … Năm … For.

examples.vi: Thứ hai , Ngày 23 Tháng 02 Năm 2024 Sunday, May
Chủ Nhật , Ngày 12 Tháng 05 Năm 2023 Tuesday, April 1st, 2022 Thứ ba , Ngày 01 Tháng 04 Năm 2022

sections.heading: ASKING ABOUT DATES IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: When you want to make the question about dates, you just need to add the question word “mấy” after “thứ” / “ngày” / “tháng”/ “năm” like this: Thứ mấy ? What day? Ngày mấy ? What date? Tháng mấy ? What month? Năm mấy ? What year? For.

Ngày mai là ngày mấy ? What date is

sections.heading: TELLING TIME IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: It’s important to learn how to give time in Vietnamese. In this lesson, we will help you to master telling time in this language in a few minutes! Let’s start!.

sections.heading: NUMBER REVIEW
sections.explanation: In order to learn how to tell time in Vietnamese, it’s important to learn about Vietnamese Numbers here. For now, let’s brush up counting from 1 to 60 in Vietnamese: Number Reading Number Reading Number Reading 1 Một 21 Hai mươi mốt 41 Bốn mươi mốt 2 Hai 22 Hai mươi hai 42 Bốn mươi hai 3 Ba 23 Hai mươi ba 43 Bốn mươi ba 4 Bốn 24 Hai mươi bốn 44 Bốn mươi bốn 5 Năm 25 Hai mươi lăm 45 Bốn mươi lăm 6 Sáu 26 Hai mươi sáu 46 Bốn mươi sáu 7 Bảy 27 Hai mươi bảy 47 Bốn mươi bảy 8 Tám 28 Hai mươi tám 48 Bốn mươi tám 9 Chín 29 Hai mươi chín 49 Bốn mươi chín 10 Mười 30 Ba mươi 50 Năm mươi 11 Mười một 31 Ba mươi mốt 51 Năm mươi mốt 12 Mười hai 32 Ba mươi hai 52 Năm mươi hai 13 Mười ba 33 Ba mươi ba 53 Năm mươi ba 14 Mười bốn 34 Ba mươi bón 54 Năm mươi bốn 15 Mười lăm 35 Ba mươi lăm 55 Năm mươi lăm 16 Mười sáu 36 Ba mươi sáu 56 Năm mươi sáu 17 Mười bảy 37 Ba mươi bảy 57 Năm mươi bảy 18 Mười tám 38 Ba mươi tám 58 Năm mươi tám 19 Mười chín 39 Ba mươi chín 59 Năm mươi chín 20 Hai mươi 40 Bốn mươi 60 Sáu mươi.

items.title: Expressing "because" in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi làm việc vì gia đình!

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY BECAUSE IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: This is one of the easiest Vietnamese grammars. Let’s go through this lesson in a few minutes with us and learn how to express “because” in Vietnamese. “Vì” / “Do” / “Bởi vì” are expressions that mean “because” in Vietnamese.

examples.vi: “Vì” / “Do” / “Bởi vì” are

sections.heading: BECAUSE WITH NOUNS / PRONOUNS
sections.explanation: You add NOUN behind “vì” / “do”/ “bởi vì” to indicate a cause or a reason. Vietnamese personal pronouns here! PATTERN Vì / Do/ Bởi vì + Noun / Personal pronouns For.

examples.vi: Vì anh! Because of you! Do em! Because of you! Bởi vì chị! Because of you! Tôi làm việc vì gia đình! I go to work
Chuyến bay bị huỷ do thời tiết! The
examples.en: flight was cancelled because of the weather

examples.vi: Bởi vì đồng tiền, cô ấy có thể làm tất cả! She does

sections.heading: BECAUSE WITH ADJECTIVES
sections.explanation: You add ADJECTIVE behind “vì” / “do”/ “bởi vì” to indicate a cause or a reason. PATTERN Vì / Do / Bởi vì + Adjective For.

examples.vi: Tôi ăn vì đói I eat
Tôi uống vì khát I
examples.en: drink because I am thirsty Anh

examples.vi: ấy đang run run do lạnh He is
ấy đổ mồ hôi do nóng nực He is
Chị ấy ngủ bởi vì mệt She
Chị ấy cáu bẩn bởi vì căng thẳng She is

sections.heading: BECAUSE WITH NÊN
sections.explanation: In order to express “because…so…” (or so-called as cause-result relationship), you can use this sentence structure: PATTERN Vì / Do / Bởi vì + (cause) + nên (result) For.

examples.vi: Vì tôi xinh đẹp nên anh ấy yêu tôi Because I am
Vì trời nóng nên tôi đổ mồ hôi Because the
examples.en: weather is hot, I sweat a lot Do

examples.vi: tôi dậy trễ nên tôi trễ chuyến bay Because I woke up
examples.en: late, I missed the flight View More Do

examples.vi: tôi không học bài nên tôi thi rớt Because I
examples.en: didn’t study, I was failed at exam

examples.vi: Bởi vì xấu xí nên tôi chưa có chồng Because I am
examples.en: ugly, I don’t have a husband yet

examples.vi: Bởi vì căng thẳng nên tôi không làm việc được. Because I am

faqs.question: How to say because in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Vì” / “Do” / “Bởi vì” are expressions that mean “because” in Vietnamese. You add NOUN behind “vì” / “do”/ “bởi vì” to indicate a cause or a reason. Vì / Do/ Bởi vì + Noun / Personal pronouns For example: Vì anh! Because of you! Tôi làm việc vì gia đình! I go to work because of my family! — You can also add ADJECTIVE behind “vì” / “do”/ “bởi vì” to indicate a cause or a reason. Vì / Do / Bởi vì + Adjective For example: Tôi ăn vì đói . I eat because I am hungry Tôi uống vì khát .

faqs.question: How to say ” you ” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: When indicating “You” with no specific gende r and “You” is at the same position level as yours, you can use: Bạn When indicating “You” with no specific gende r and “You” is at a lower position level than yours, you call: Con or Cháu (as a child/ a grandchild) Em (as a younger brother/a younger sister) When indicating “You” with male-gende r and “You” is at a higher position level than yours, you call: Anh (as an older brother) Chú (as an uncle) Ông (as a very old man) When indicating “You” with female gender and “You” is at a higher position level than yours, you call: Chị (as an older sister) Cô (as an aunt) Bà (as a very old woman)

extracted_patterns: Vì / Do/ Bởi vì + Noun / Personal pronouns

items.title: Expressing "but" in Vietnamese
items.pattern: Clause 1 + nhưng / nhưng mà / mà + Clause 2

example.vi: Việt Nam có đồ ăn ngon nhưng thời tiết rất nóng

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY BUT IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, you can express “but” in only one word which is “NHƯNG”. Let’s have a look in this lesson to learn how to use it properly!.

examples.vi: “NHƯNG”. Let’s have a look in this
examples.en: lesson to learn how to use it properly!

sections.heading: BUT IN VIETNAMESE WITH NHƯNG
sections.explanation: You can add “NHƯNG” between 2 contrast clauses in 1 sentence or 2 contrast sentences. There are other synonyms for “NHƯNG” such as “ NHƯNG MÀ” / “MÀ”. For.

examples.vi: Việt Nam có đồ ăn ngon nhưng thời tiết rất nóng Vietnam has good
examples.en: cuisine but the weather is too hot!

examples.vi: Tôi thích ăn ngọt. Nhưng đó không phải là kem. I love
Bánh mì đắt mà ngon thì OK. It’s okay for banh mi to be
Tuyết đẹp nhưng mà lạnh quá! Snow is
Gà chiên
Nhưng mà nó béo quá! Fried
examples.en: chicken is delicious. But it’s too greasy!

examples.vi: Cơm ngon mà cay quá! Rice is

sections.note: “MÀ” can only be used between different clauses in a sentence. 1 + nhưng / nhưng mà / mà + Clause 2 PATTERN #2 Sentence 1. Nhưng / nhưng mà + Sentence 2

sections.heading: TUY…NHƯNG / DÙ…NHƯNG
sections.explanation: In order to express the contrast statements in a sentence, you can use phrases such as “Tuy…nhưng” or “Dù…nhưng” with a structure like this: 1 + nhưng + clause 2 For.

examples.vi: không đẹp trai nhưng anh giàu Even
examples.en: though he is not handsome, he is rich Tuy anh

examples.vi: giàu nhưng anh vẫn mắc nợ Even
examples.en: though he is rich, he is in debt Tuy anh

examples.vi: mắc nợ nhưng anh không buồn Even
examples.en: though he is in debt, he is not depressed

faqs.question: How to use NHƯNG for “but “in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can add “NHƯNG” between 2 contrast clauses in 1 sentence or 2 contrast sentences. There are other synonyms for “NHƯNG” such as “ NHƯNG MÀ” / “MÀ” . Note: “MÀ” can only be used between different clauses in a sentence. Clause 1 + nhưng / nhưng mà / mà + Clause 2 For example: Việt Nam có đồ ăn ngon nhưng thời tiết rất nóng Vietnam has good cuisine but the weather is too hot! Bánh mì đắt mà ngon thì OK.

faqs.question: What are some basic sentence structures?
faqs.answer: The most basic sentence structures in Vietnamese are as follow: Subject + “là” + Noun Tôi là David // I am David Subject + Adjective Anh ấy xấu // He is ugly Subject + Verb + Object Chị ấy học Tiếng Việt // She studies Vietnamese

extracted_patterns: Clause 1 + nhưng / nhưng mà / mà + Clause 2

example.vi: Tôi thích con mèo và con chó

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY AND IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: And in Vietnamese can be expressed using one simple word: và. We teach you 4 ways to use it properly with the corresponding sentence structures.

sections.heading: VÀ + NOUNS
sections.explanation: Let’s first have a look at how to use và with nouns.

examples.vi: Họ muốn du lịch ở Paris và London They want to
Chúng tôi uống nước cam và nước mía We
examples.en: drink orange juice and sugar cane juice Em

examples.vi: sẽ đi du lịch thứ 2 và thứ 3 I will
Chị ấy nên lắng nghe và nói chuyện với bạn She
examples.en: needs to listen and talk to you

examples.vi: Chúng tôi không thích xem phim và nghe nhạc cùng nhau We
examples.en: don’t love to watch movie and listen to music together

examples.vi: Họ thích mua xe và lái xe They love to buy car and
ấy tức giận và khó chịu He is
Cái áo này rẻ và đẹp This
examples.en: shirt is cheap and nice Em

examples.vi: rất vui và hạnh phúc I am

sections.heading: VÀ + PRONOUNS
sections.explanation: Finally, let’s have a look at how to use và with personal pronouns: 1 + và + Pronoun 2 Anh và em yêu nhau You and me love each other Mẹ và con ăn cơm với ba (You and me) Let’s have a meal with dad! Chúng tôi và họ giống nhau We and them are same.

examples.vi: ấy và vợ không thích uống rượu He and his wife
examples.en: don’t like to drink wine Em

examples.vi: và anh đi chơi nhé! (You and me) Let’s hang out! Chị và em nấu ăn nhé! (You and me) Let’s

faqs.question: How to say and in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: And in Vietnamese can be used using the word và .

faqs.question: How to use và with nouns?
faqs.answer: The correct structure to use và with nouns is the following: Noun 1 + và + Noun 2 Tôi thích con mèo và con chó. I love cat and dog.

faqs.question: The correct structure to use và with verbs is the following: Verb 1 + và + Verb 2 How to say with in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: With in Vietnamese can be said by using multiple words. The most common sentence structures are: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau . I come home together with my mom — Learn more about with in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: Where to find more lessons like this?
faqs.answer: If you prefer to study from home, check out our online courses .

example.vi: Tôi nên đi học

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY SHOULD IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, the concept of “should” is expressed us ing a specific structure, easy to learn in the early stages of language learning. We teach you how to use “nên ” and “Nên làm gì” in every day conversation.

examples.vi: “nên ” and “Nên làm gì” in

sections.heading: NÊN || SHOULD
sections.explanation: Expressing should in Vietnamese can be done by using the word nên right before the verb. tiếng mỗi ngày She should sleep 8 hours everyday.
sections.pattern: Subject + nên + verb Tôi nên đi học I should go to school Anh ấy nên đi làm He should go to work Chị ấy nên ngủ 8

examples.vi: Chúng tôi nên nghỉ ngơi We
ấy nên ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối She
nên học Tiếng Việt mỗi ngày You

sections.heading: NEGATIVE FORM
sections.explanation: To create a negative sentence and turn should into shouldn’t, add “không” before “nên” : tiếng mỗi ngày She shouldn’t sleep 8 hours everyday.
sections.pattern: Subject + không nên + verb Tôi không nên đi học I shouldn’t go to school Anh ấy không nên đi làm He shouldn’t go to work Chị ấy không nên ngủ 8

examples.vi: Chúng tôi không nên nghỉ ngơi We
ấy không nên ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối She
không nên học Tiếng Việt mỗi ngày You

sections.heading: NÊN LÀM GÌ? || WHAT SHOULD I DO?
sections.explanation: This is how you can ask a question with “nên”: tiếng mỗi ngày What should she do everyday? She should sleep 8 hours everyday.
sections.pattern: Subject + nên + làm gì? Bạn nên làm gì mỗi ngày? Tôi nên đi học mỗi ngày What should you do everyday? I should go to school Anh ấy nên làm gì mỗi ngày? Anh ấy nên đi làm mỗi ngày What should he do everyday? He should go to work Chị ấy nên làm gì mỗi ngày? Chị ấy nên ngủ 8

examples.vi: Bạn nên làm gì mỗi ngày? Chúng tôi nên nghỉ ngơi mỗi ngày What
examples.en: should you do everyday? We should take a rest Em

examples.vi: ấy nên làm gì lúc 7h00 tối? Em ấy nên ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối What
examples.en: should she do every 7pm? She should have dinner at 7pm Anh

examples.vi: nên làm gì mỗi ngày? Anh nên học Tiếng Việt mỗi ngày What
examples.en: should I do everyday? You should learn Vietnamese everyday

faqs.question: How to say should in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Expressing should in Vietnamese can be done by using the word nên right before the verb. Subject + nên + verb Tôi nên đi học. I should go to school Anh ấy nên đi làm.

faqs.question: He should go to work How to say should not in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To create a negative sentence and turn should into shouldn’t, add “không” before “nên” : Subject + không nên + verb Tôi không nên đi học. I shouldn’t go to school Anh ấy không nên đi làm.

faqs.question: What should you do everyday?
faqs.answer: Tôi nên đi học mỗi ngày.

faqs.question: I should go to school Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. Learn more about the Vietnamese sentence structure here.

sections.heading: EXPRESSING VIETNAMESE SIMILARITIES
sections.explanation: The word “ giống như ” in Vietnamese is used to make comparisons or indicate similarity between two things. In this lesson, we will learn how to use “giống như” to express concepts like “like”, “as”, or “similar to”.

faqs.question: How do you express “similar to” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express “similar to,” you can use giống như or tương tự như . For example, “Cô ấy giống như chị của cô ấy” (She is similar to her sister).

faqs.question: Can “giống” be used for both people and things?
faqs.answer: Yes, giống can be used for both people and things to indicate similarities, such as “Quyển sách này giống quyển kia” (This book is similar to that one).

faqs.question: Any difference between “tương tự” and “giống”?
faqs.answer: Yes, tương tự (similar) is often used for formal or abstract comparisons, while giống (like) is more common in everyday speech. Tương tự implies a resemblance but not an exact match, while giống suggests a closer likeness.

faqs.question: How to say more than in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: When you want to compare two things to highlight the superiority of its characteristics such as size, quality, texture, etc in Vietnamese, use this structure: Subject 1 + (not) + Adjective + hơn (more) + Subject 2 Bác sĩ giàu hơn cô giáo. Doctor is richer than teacher Anh ấy cao hơn cô ấy. He is taller than her.

items.title: Making requests in Vietnamese

example.vi: Anh vui lòng không hút thuốc ở đây ạ!

sections.heading: MAKING REQUESTS IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Making requests in Vietnamese is as important as learning how to ask questions! We teach you a few sentence structures you will hear frequently in your Vietnamese studies , so make sure to bookmark this page and come back to it whenever you need a quick review.

sections.heading: COULD YOU…FOR ME PLEASE?
sections.explanation: To ask for help / ask for a favour or ask for someone to do something for you, you can use the verb could có thể. Pay attention to the sentence structure below: Anh có thể mua bánh cho em được không ạ ? Could you buy a biscuit for me please? Chị có thể gửi tin nhắn cho em được không ạ ? Could you send a message for me please? Bạn có thể đưa tờ giấy này cho mình được không ạ ? Could you give me this paper please?.
sections.pattern: Subject + có thể + verb + cho + pronoun + được không ạ? See more details about the structure: Subject: the person you are asking Có thể: could Pronoun: 1st personal pronoun, such as mình, tôi, anh, em, etc Được không ạ: please

examples.vi: Ông có thể dạy Tiếng Việt cho con được không ạ ? Could you
Bạn có thể chỉ đường đến siêu thị cho mình được không ạ ? Could you show me

sections.heading: PLEASE…!
sections.explanation: To give a polite request to the customer in a formal way , you can use “Vui lòng…ạ!” by following this pattern below:
sections.pattern: Subject + vui lòng + verb + ạ! Anh vui lòng không hút thuốc ở đây ạ ! Please do not smoke here, sir! Bạn vui lòng order món ở đây ạ! Please take an order here, friend! Chị vui lòng xuất giấy tờ ạ! Please show me your papers, madam!

faqs.question: How to make requests in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To ask for help / ask for a favour or ask for someone to do something for you, you can use the verb could có thể .

faqs.question: How to use vui lòng in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To give a polite request to the customer in a formal way , you can use “Vui lòng…ạ!” by following this pattern below: Subject + vui lòng + verb + ạ! Anh vui lòng không hút thuốc ở đây ạ ! Please do not smoke here, sir!

faqs.question: What is the Vietnamese command form?
faqs.answer: The command form in Vietnamese uses multiple words: You use “…đi!” when ordering or commanding in an aggressive way. You use “…nào!” when ordering or commanding in an encouraging way Không được means to not be allowed to do something . You use “Đừng…” when you want to command someone not to do something . You use “Xin hãy…” when you want to ask for help or favour (please) but in a very desperate way . — Check out sentence examples in our lesson about the Vietnamese command form .

faqs.question: Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. — Learn more about Vietnamese sentence structure here.

items.title: Expressing "as soon as" in Vietnamese
items.pattern: Ngay khi + clause A, clause B

example.vi: Ngay khi thấy ăn trộm, tôi báo cảnh sát lập tức!

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY AS SOON AS IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this grammar lesson we teach you how to express “as soon as” in Vietnamese. There are two common “as soon as” expressions in Vietnamese, which are “Ngay khi” and “Vừa mới”. Let’s have a look.

examples.vi: “Vừa mới” . Let’s have a

sections.heading: AS SOON AS WITH NGAY KHI
sections.explanation: To express as soon as in Vietnamese you can use Ngay khi. It is used to emphasize that one action happens immediately after another. It connects two events that are tightly linked in time, with the second event happening right after the first.

examples.vi: bị đứt tay, tôi khóc thét! I
examples.en: cried out loud as soon as my finger was cut Ngay khi

examples.vi: bị hiểu lầm, tôi giải thích I
examples.en: explained as soon as I was misunderstood

sections.heading: AS SOON AS WITH VỪA MỚI
sections.explanation: Another slightly different way to say as soon as in Vietnamese is Vừa mới. It also indicates that one action happens right after another, but it conveys a sense that something just happened, or only a short time has passed. It is often used to express actions that happened very recently or to emphasize that something has just been completed.
sections.pattern: Subject + vừa mới + verb, clause Tôi vừa mới thấy ăn trộm, tôi báo cảnh sát lập tức! I called a police as soon as I found a thief Anh ấy vừa mới bị đau bụng, anh ấy uống thuốc He took a medicine as soon as he had a stomach ache

examples.vi: Cô ấy vừa mới ngồi xuống, cô ấy nhận 1 cuộc gọi She got a call as soon as she just sat down Tôi vừa mới bị đứt tay, tôi khóc thét! I
examples.en: cried out loud as soon as my finger was cut

examples.vi: Tôi vừa mới bị hiểu lầm, tôi giải thích I
examples.en: explained as soon as I was misunderstood

faqs.question: How to say as soon as in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express as soon as in Vietnamese you can use Ngay khi . It is used to emphasize that one action happens immediately after another . It connects two events that are tightly linked in time, with the second event happening right after the first. Ngay khi + clause A, clause B Ngay khi thấy ăn trộm, tôi báo cảnh sát lập tức! I called a police as soon as I found a thief — Another slightly different way to say as soon as in Vietnamese is Vừa mới . It is often used to express actions that happened very recently or to emphasize that something has just been completed. Subject + vừa mới + verb, clause Tôi vừa mới thấy ăn trộm, tôi báo cảnh sát lập tức!

faqs.question: I called a police as soon as I found a thief Ngay khi vs vừa mới , what difference?
faqs.answer: Ngay khi is used to emphasize that one action happens immediately after another . Vừa mới is often used to express actions that happened very recently or to emphasize that something has just been completed.

faqs.question: How to say almost in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Almost in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words gần như and hầu như . — Learn more about almost in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: How to say or in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Or in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words hoặc and hay . In Vietnamese you will use the word “hoặc” to give more than two options in a sentence . N1/ V1/ A1 + hoặc + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hoặc + N3/ V3/ A3) Cái bàn hoặc cái ghế Table or chair Another way to say “or” in Vietnamese is with the word hay . It is used between nouns, verbs and adjectives. The two words hay and hoặc can be use interchangeably and equally in any situation.

faqs.question: N1/ V1/ A1 + hay + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hay + N3/ V3/ A3) Con gái hay con trai Girl or boy?
faqs.answer: — Learn more about how to say or in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: Where to find more lessons like this?
faqs.answer: Check out our Vietnamese Grammar Bank , where you will find free lessons for levels A1 to B1. If you prefer to study from home, check out our online courses .

example.vi: Tôi chưa ăn trưa.

sections.heading: NOT YET/ALREADY IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing actions that have or have not been completed can be done using specific words like chưa (not yet) and rồi (already). Let’s explore how to use them correctly!.

examples.vi: chưa (not yet) and rồi
examples.en: (already). Let’s explore how to use them correctly!

sections.heading: CHƯA = NOT YET
sections.explanation: The word chưa is used to express that something has not been done yet, or an action has not been completed. It is equivalent to “ not yet ” in English. For.

examples.vi: Tôi chưa ăn trưa. I
ấy chưa hoàn thành bài tập về nhà. He
Họ chưa đến. They
Cô ấy chưa gọi cho tôi. She
Tôi chưa đọc cuốn sách đó. I
Chúng tôi chưa quyết định. We

sections.heading: RỒI = ALREADY
sections.explanation: The word rồi is used to indicate that something has already been done or completed. It corresponds to “ already ” in English and is used for actions that are finished. For.

examples.vi: Tôi ăn trưa rồi I have
ấy hoàn thành bài tập về nhà rồi He has
Họ đến rồi They have
Cô ấy gọi cho tôi rồi She has
Tôi đọc cuốn sách đó rồi I have read that book
Chúng tôi quyết định rồi We have

sections.heading: …CHƯA?
sections.explanation: This pattern is used to express the question of “ Have you done…yet? ” PATTERN #1 Subject + (đã) + verb + chưa? PATTERN #2 Yes: Rồi, subject + verb + rồi PATTERN #3 Not yet: Chưa, subject + chưa + verb For.

examples.vi: Bạn (đã) ăn trưa chưa ? – Rồi , tôi ăn trưa rồi – Chưa, tôi chưa ăn Have you had
examples.en: lunch? -Yes, I’ve already eaten lunch -No, I’ve not eaten lunch yet Anh

examples.vi: ngủ chưa ? – Rồi , anh ngủ rồi – Chưa , anh chưa ngủ Have you
examples.en: slept? -Yes, I slept -No, I’ve not slept yet Em

examples.vi: đi làm chưa ? – Rồi , em ở công ty rồi – Chưa , em chưa đi làm Have you gone to
examples.en: work?-Yes, I am already at office -No, I’ve not gone yet Ba

examples.vi: dậy chưa ? – Rồi , ba súc miệng rồi – Chưa , ba chưa dậy Have you
examples.en: woken up dad? -Yes, I’ve brushed my teeth already -No, I’ve not woken up yet

examples.vi: Bạn tốt nghiệp chưa ? – Rồi , tôi tốt nghiệp năm ngoái rồi – Chưa , tôi chưa tốt nghiệp Have you
examples.en: graduated? -Yes, I graduated last year -No, I’ve not graduated yet

examples.vi: Chị ấy nấu ăn chưa ? – Rồi , chị ấy nấu ăn rồi – Chưa , chị ấy chưa nấu ăn Have you
examples.en: cooked yet? -Yes, I’ve cooked already -No, I’ve not cooked yet

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “chưa” and “rồi” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Chưa” is used to indicate that something has not happened yet , while “rồi” confirms that something has already happened .

faqs.question: Can I use “chưa” without “rồi” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes, “chưa” can stand alone to express incompletion. For example: Tôi chưa ăn (I haven’t eaten yet).

faqs.question: Add “chưa” at the end of the sentence to ask if something has happened: Bạn ăn chưa?
Have you eaten yet?
Can I use “chưa” in positive statements?
faqs.answer: No, “chưa” is used in negative or interrogative contexts to indicate an action has not been done yet.

faqs.question: Is it necessary to use “rồi” in affirmative answers?
faqs.answer: While not always required, “rồi” is commonly used in affirmative answers to clearly state that the action has already occurred: Tôi ăn rồi (I’ve already eaten).

items.title: Expressing desire in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi muốn tiền

sections.heading: WISH & WANT IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: To express desire in Vietnamese we can use several sentence structures, to either say “want”, “wish” or “hope” something. We guide you through the uses of muốn, ước and mong.

examples.vi: muốn, ước and

sections.heading: MUỐN || TO WANT TO
sections.explanation: “Muốn” in Vietnamese means “To want something” or “To want to do something ” in English. You can use “muốn” by following this pattern below:
sections.pattern: Subject + muốn + noun / verb / adjective Tôi muốn tiền I want money Anh ấy muốn 10 ngôi nhà He wants 10 houses Chị ấy muốn uống nước She wants to drink water

examples.vi: Chúng tôi muốn về nhà We want to go home Anh ấy muốn khoẻ mạnh He
ấy muốn xinh đẹp She

sections.heading: MUỐN || DO NOT WANT TO
sections.explanation: You can add “không” before “muốn” in order to express desire using in the negative .
sections.pattern: Subject + không + muốn + noun / verb / adjective Tôi không muốn tiền I don’t want money Anh ấy không muốn 10 ngôi nhà He doesn’t want 10 houses Chị ấy không muốn uống nước She doesn’t want to drink water

examples.vi: ấy không muốn khoẻ mạnh He
examples.en: doesn’t want to be strong Em

sections.heading: ƯỚC || TO WISH
sections.explanation: “Ước” in Vietnamese means “To wish” in English. You can use “ước” by following this pattern below:
sections.pattern: Subject + ước + clause Tôi ước tôi có tiền I wish I could have money Anh ấy ước anh ấy có thể mua 10 ngôi nhà He wishes he could buy 10 houses Chị ấy ước chị ấy có thể uống nước She wishes she could drink water

sections.heading: MONG || TO HOPE
sections.explanation: “Mong” in Vietnamese means “To hope” in English. You can use “mong” by following this pattern below:
sections.pattern: Subject + mong + clause Tôi mong tôi có tiền I hope I have money Anh ấy mong anh ấy có thể mua 10 ngôi nhà He hopes he can buy 10 houses Chị ấy mong chị ấy có thể uống nước She hopes she can drink water

faqs.question: He wants 10 houses How to use Ước in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Ước” in Vietnamese means “To wish” in English. You can use “ước” by following this pattern below: Subject + ước + clause Tôi ước tôi có tiền. I wish I could have money Anh ấy ước anh ấy có thể mua 10 ngôi nhà.

faqs.question: He wishes he could buy 10 houses How to use Mong in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Mong” in Vietnamese means “To hope” in English. You can use “mong” by following this pattern below: Subject + mong + clause Tôi mong tôi có tiền. I hope I have money Anh ấy mong anh ấy có thể mua 10 ngôi nhà.

items.title: How to say "very" in Vietnamese

example.vi: Rất nóng! Rất lạnh!

sections.heading: DEGREE ADVERBS IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: How would you say “very hot”, “too hot” or “extremely hot” in Vietnamese? We teach you how to use the degree adverbs “too/very/extremely” in Vietnamese, using rất , quá and more.

examples.vi: rất , quá and

sections.heading: RẤT TO EXPRESS VERY, TOO
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, in order to express “very” or “too”, you can follow this pattern below: PATTERN Rất + adjective! Rất nóng! → Việt Nam rất nóng! Too hot! → Vietnam is too hot! Rất lạnh! → Mùa đông rất lạnh! Too cold → Winter is too cold!.

examples.vi: Bánh mì rất
examples.en: ngon! Very delicious → Banh mi is very delicious

examples.vi: Rất đẹp! → Thiên nhiên rất đẹp! Very
Rất tốt! → Bài kiểm tra rất tốt! Very good → The test was very good Rất đắng! → Cà phê rất đắng! Too

sections.heading: QUÁ TO EXPRESS VERY, TOO
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, in order to express “very” or “too”, you can also use “ quá ” (same as “ rất ”). However, the pattern is different from “rất” structure: PATTERN Adjective + quá! Nóng quá ! → Việt Nam nóng quá ! Too hot! → Vietnam is too hot! Lạnh quá ! → Mùa đông lạnh quá ! Too cold → Winter is too cold!.

examples.vi: quá ! → Bánh mì ngon quá ! Very
examples.en: delicious → Banh mi is very delicious

examples.vi: Đẹp quá ! → Thiên nhiên đẹp quá ! Very
Tốt quá ! → Bài kiểm tra tốt quá ! Very good → The test was very good Đắng quá ! → Cà phê đắng quá ! Too

sections.heading: CỰC KỲ TO EXPRESS EXTREMELY
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, in order to express “extremely”, you can follow this pattern below: PATTERN Cực kỳ + adjective! Cực kỳ nóng! → Việt Nam cực kỳ nóng! Extremely hot! → Vietnam is extremely hot! Cực kỳ lạnh! → Mùa đông cực kỳ lạnh! Extremely cold → Winter is extremely cold!.

examples.vi: Cực kỳ
Bánh mì cực kỳ

faqs.question: How to say too, very in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, in order to express “very” or “too”, you can follow this pattern below: Rất + adjective! Rất nóng! → Việt Nam rất nóng! Too hot! → Vietnam is too hot!

faqs.question: How to say extremely in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, in order to express “extremely”, you can follow this pattern below: Cực kỳ + adjective! Cực kỳ nóng! → Việt Nam cực kỳ nóng! Extremely hot! → Vietnam is extremely hot!

faqs.question: What is quá in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yo express too, very in Vietnamese can also use “ quá ” (same as “ rất ”). However, the pattern is different from “rất” structure: Adjective + quá! Nóng quá ! → Việt Nam nóng quá ! Too hot! → Vietnam is too hot!

faqs.question: How to say must in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, there is only one word to express something that you must do or have to do. The word is “phải” . Subject + phải + verb Tôi phải đi học. I have to go to school Anh ấy phải đi làm. He has to go to work — Learn more about must in Vietnamese here.

Could you please tell me the way to…?
For example, “Làm ơn cho tôi hỏi đường đến nhà ga?

How to say go straight and turn left?
faqs.answer: “Go straight” is Đi thẳng , and “turn left” is Rẽ trái or Quẹo trái .

faqs.question: Is there a polite way to ask for directions?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can use polite phrases like Làm ơn (please) or Xin lỗi (excuse me) before asking for directions, e.g., “

What are the cardinal directions in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The cardinal directions are Bắc (north), Nam (south), Đông (east), and Tây (west).

sections.heading: CAN, BE ABLE TO, BE CAPABLE ETC
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express ability or capacity in Vietnamese using common phrases like có thể , biết , giỏi , and có khả năng. These structures allow you to talk about what someone can do, knows how to do, or is skilled at, in both casual and formal contexts.

examples.vi: có thể , biết , giỏi , and có khả năng . These
examples.en: structures allow you to talk about what someone can do, knows how to do, or is skilled at, in both casual and formal contexts.

sections.heading: CÓ THỂ… = CAN
sections.explanation: “ Có thể ” means “ can ” or “ be able to ” and is used to indicate the ability to perform an action. tiếng Việt I can speak Vietnamese Cô ấy có thể bơi She can swim very well Anh ấy có thể chơi đàn piano He can play the piano. Chúng ta có thể đi công viên vào ngày mai. We can go to the park tomorrow Họ có thể hoàn thành dự án vào thứ Sáu They can finish the project by Friday Tôi có thể giúp bạn với bài tập về nhà. I can help you with your homework.

Chúng ta biết chạy xe máy We can ride scooter Họ biết đếm They can count Tôi biết nấu ăn I can cook.

sections.explanation: This phrase is used to express potential or capability in performing an action. tiếng Việt I can speak Vietnamese Cô ấy có khả năng bơi She can swim very well Anh ấy có khả năng chơi đàn piano He can play the piano. Chúng ta có khả năng chạy xe máy We can ride scooter Họ có khả năng đếm They can count Tôi có khả năng nấu ăn I can cook.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “có thể” and “có khả năng”?
faqs.answer: “ Có thể ” usually expresses the possibility or permission to do something, while “ có khả năng ” emphasizes ability or capacity , often in a more formal or evaluative context.

faqs.question: When should I use “giỏi” instead of “biết”?
faqs.answer: Use “ giỏi ” when you want to say someone is skilled or good at something. Use “ biết ” simply to indicate that someone knows how to do something.

faqs.question: Can these words be used interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Not always. While they all relate to capacity or ability, they differ in tone, formality , and specific meaning . For example, “giỏi” is often used to praise, whereas “biết” is neutral and factual.

faqs.question: How do I make negative statements using these expressions?
faqs.answer: You can add “ không ” in front of the expression. For example: “Tôi không biết nấu ăn.” (I don’t know how to cook.) “Anh ấy không giỏi tiếng Anh.” (He’s not good at English.)

faqs.question: Are these expressions commonly used in daily conversation?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially “ có thể “, “ biết “, and “ giỏi ” are very commonly used in both formal and informal contexts. “ Có khả năng ” is more formal but still useful in written or professional Vietnamese.

sections.heading: MAKING COMPARISONS IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: There are four basic comparison structures in Vietnamese: 1 – “Hơn” ~ More 2 – “Ít hơn” ~ Less 3 – “Bằng” ~ As…as 4 – “Nhất” ~ The most! Let’s start!.

examples.vi: “Hơn” ~ More 2 – “Ít hơn” ~ Less 3 – “Bằng” ~ As…as 4 – “Nhất” ~ The

sections.heading: HƠN = MORE THAN
sections.explanation: When you want to compare two things to highlight the superiority of its characteristics such as size, quality, texture, etc in Vietnamese, use this structure:
sections.pattern: Subject 1 + (not) + Adjective + hơn (more) + Subject 2 Bác sĩ giàu hơn cô giáo Doctor is richer than teacher Anh ấy cao hơn cô ấy He is taller than her Con hổ dữ hơn con thỏ Tiger is more aggressive than rabbit

faqs.answer: When you want to compare two things to highlight the superiority of its characteristics such as size, quality, texture, etc in Vietnamese, use this structure: Subject 1 + (not) + Adjective + hơn (more) + Subject 2 Bác sĩ giàu hơn cô giáo. faqs.answer: hen you want to compare things on the down side between two things in Vietnamese and express “less”, use this structure: Subject 1 + ít (little) + Adjective + hơn (more) + Subject 2 Tôi ít thông minh hơn cô ấy. I am less smart than her Cafe sữa ít đắng hơn cafe đen.

faqs.question: Milk coffee is less bitter than black coffee How to say as…as in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: When you want to compare two things that have the same characteristics such as size, quality, texture, etc in Vietnamese, use this structure: Subject 1 + (not) + Adjective + bằng (same) + Subject 2 Bác sĩ giàu bằng cô giáo. Doctor is as rich as teacher Anh ấy cao bằng cô ấy.

faqs.question: He is as tall as her Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object.

sections.heading: THE COMMAND FORM IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: To command someone or insist someone do something in Vietnamese you can use đi , nào and a few other words. Let’s have a look at all of them together.

examples.vi: đi , nào and a few
examples.en: other words. Let’s have a look at all of them together.

sections.heading: COMMAND FORM WITH ĐI /NÀO
sections.explanation: Both “…đi” and “…nào!” are used to order or command the person whom you are talking to. Just a little difference: You use “…đi!” when ordering or commanding in an aggressive way. You use “…nào!” when ordering or commanding in an encouraging way (to push someone or boost someone up to do something). PATTERN Verb + đi! / nào! Cút đi ! Get out! Đi ngủ đi ! Go to bed! Ăn đi ! Eat!.

faqs.question: How to use the Vietnamese command form?
faqs.answer: The command form in Vietnamese uses multiple words: You use “…đi!” when ordering or commanding in an aggressive way. You use “…nào!” when ordering or commanding in an encouraging way Không được means to not be allowed to do something . You use “Đừng…” when you want to command someone not to do something . You use “Xin hãy…” when you want to ask for help or favour (please) but in a very desperate way . Check out sentence examples in our lesson.

faqs.question: How to use đi in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese you use “…đi!” when ordering or commanding in an aggressive way. Verb + đi! Examples: Cút đi ! Get out! Đi ngủ đi ! Go to bed! Ăn đi ! Eat!

faqs.question: How to use Không được in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Không được means to not be allowed to do something . Không được + verb Examples: Không được ngủ No sleeping! Không được ăn No eating! Không được uống No drinking!

faqs.question: How to use Đừng for don’t in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You use “Đừng…” when you want to command someone not to do something : Đừng (Don’t) + verb Examples: Đừng ngủ Don’t sleep! Đừng ăn Don’t eat! Đừng uống Don’t drink!

faqs.question: How to form conditional sentences?
faqs.answer: Conditional sentences in Vietnamese are typically formed using “nếu” (if) at the beginning of the condition clause, followed by the result clause. For example, “Nếu trời mưa, tôi sẽ ở nhà” (If it rains, I will stay home).

faqs.question: Can conditional sentences in Vietnamese omit the word “nếu”?
faqs.answer: Yes, in some cases, “nếu” can be omitted if the context makes the condition clear, especially in informal speech. For example, “Trời mưa thì tôi sẽ ở nhà” (If it rains, I will stay home) omits “nếu.”

faqs.question: Is the word order in Vietnamese conditional sentences the same as in English?
faqs.answer: Yes, the word order in Vietnamese conditional sentences is similar to English, with the condition clause first (starting with “nếu”) and the result clause following. However, these clauses can also be reversed, just like in English.

faqs.question: Can conditional sentences in Vietnamese omit the word “nếu”?
faqs.answer: Yes, in some cases, “nếu” can be omitted if the context makes the condition clear, especially in informal speech. For example, “Trời mưa thì tôi sẽ ở nhà” (If it rains, I will stay home) omits “nếu.”

extracted_patterns: Trừ khi + conditional-clause, thì (so) + subject + mới + verb

example.vi: Tôi thấy hoa vàng trên cỏ xanh

sections.heading: PERCEPTION VERBS IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In both English and Vietnamese, we use perception verbs to describe how we experience the world through our senses. These verbs help us express what we see, hear, smell, taste, and feel. In this lesson, we will explore how to use perception verbs in Vietnamese to talk about sensory experiences.

sections.heading: TÔI THẤY… = I SEE…
sections.explanation: The verb “thấy” is used to indicate “ I see ” or “ I perceive ” visually or physical things:

sections.explanation: The verb “nghe” is used for hearing sounds or noises or information which is not official .

sections.explanation: The verb “nếm thấy” is used to describe tasting food or drinks For.

sections.explanation: The verb “cảm thấy” is used for feelings or physical sensations. It can be used for emotional and sensory perceptions. For.

faqs.question: What are the most common perception verbs in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Some of the most common ones include: nghe (to hear), nhìn (to see/look), ngửi (to smell), nếm (to taste), cảm thấy (to feel), and trông có vẻ (to look like/seem).

faqs.question: Can perception verbs be used with both physical and emotional experiences?
faqs.answer: Yes. For example, cảm thấy can be used to express physical sensations ( cảm thấy lạnh – feel cold) or emotions ( cảm thấy buồn – feel sad).

faqs.question: How do I say something “looks/smells/tastes good” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can use structures like: trông ngon , ngửi thơm , or nếm rất ngon to express these ideas.

faqs.question: Are there grammar rules when using these verbs with adjectives?
faqs.answer: Yes. Often, these verbs are directly followed by an adjective. For instance, nghe hay (sounds good), trông đẹp (looks pretty), nếm lạ (tastes strange).

faqs.question: What is the difference between “trông”, “nhìn”, and “thấy”?
faqs.answer: Trông and nhìn imply deliberate looking (active perception). Thấy can be more passive (you noticed something or it came into your perception). E.g. Tôi thấy anh ấy ngoài đường (I saw him on the street).

items.title: Quantifiers in Vietnamese

example.vi: Một số nhà hàng sẽ đóng cửa vào cuối tuần

sections.heading: QUANTIFIERS IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Quantifiers in Vietnamese help indicate the amount or quantity of something, whether it’s “a few,” “some,” or “none at all.” In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use common quantifiers like một ít , một vài , nhiều , and không có accurately in everyday conversations.

sections.heading: MỘT SỐ… = A NUMBER OF…
sections.explanation: It is used to refer to “ a number of ” or “some” when talking about a few or several things, usually when the exact number is not specified. PATTERN Một số + noun.

examples.vi: Một số nhà hàng sẽ đóng cửa vào cuối tuần A
examples.en: number of restaurants will close on the weekend

examples.en: number of my friends have gotten married.

examples.vi: Tôi muốn nói chuyện với anh về một số thứ I want to talk to you
Một số điều anh còn giấu em là gì? What are a
examples.en: number of things you are still hiding from me?

examples.vi: Tôi đã bán một số căn nhà mất rồi I’ve
examples.en: already sold a number of houses

sections.heading: MỘT VÀI… = A FEW / SEVERAL OF…
sections.explanation: It is used for a small number of items, similar to “a few” or “a several of” in English.

examples.vi: Một vài nhà hàng sẽ đóng cửa vào cuối tuần A few
examples.en: restaurants will close on the weekend

Một vài bác sĩ không thích nói chuyện với bệnh nhân A few

Một vài điều anh còn giấu em là gì? What are a few

examples.vi: Tôi đã bán một vài căn nhà mất rồi I’ve

sections.heading: MỘT ÍT…= A LITTLE
sections.explanation: It is used to indicate a small quantity, similar to “ a little ” or “ some ” in English. PATTERN Một ít + noun For.

examples.vi: một ít nước được không? May I have a
Một ít quốc gia cho phép hôn nhân đồng tính A few

sections.explanation: It is used for emphasizing “ a lot of ” or “ so many ” in both countable and uncountable contexts.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “một ít” and “một chút”?
faqs.answer: Both mean “a little” or “a bit,” but “một chút” is slightly more informal and often used in spoken Vietnamese.

faqs.question: Can quantifiers like “nhiều” (a lot) go before or after the noun?
faqs.answer: In most cases, quantifiers like “nhiều” come before the noun (e.g., “nhiều người” – many people). However, in spoken language, they may sometimes appear after for emphasis.

faqs.question: How do you express “none” or “not any” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can use “không có” (not have) or “chẳng có” (not any, stronger tone) to express the absence of something.

faqs.question: Is there a difference between “một vài” and “một số”?
faqs.answer: Yes . “Một vài” means “a few” with emphasis on small quantity, while “một số” can mean “some” or “a number of,” which can imply a slightly larger, less specific quantity.

faqs.question: Do quantifiers change form based on the noun that follows?
faqs.answer: No , Vietnamese quantifiers do not change form based on the noun. However, they are often used with classifiers (e.g., “một ít nước ” vs. “một ít người “).

items.title: Simultaneous actions in Vietnamese

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE SIMULTANEOUS ACTIONS
sections.explanation: In this free grammar lesson we teach you two phrases to state or to describe two actions happening at the same time in Vietnamese.

examples.vi: “Vừa… vừa”

sections.heading: VỪA…VỪA – DO BOTH THINGS / WHILE
sections.explanation: To express two actions done at the same time by one person or one subject, you can use “vừa…vừa…” by following the pattern below:

examples.vi: ấy đang vừa tắm vừa hát He is

examples.vi: Chị ấy vừa làm mẹ vừa làm cha She is both a mom and a dad
Chị ấy đang vừa nhắn tin vừa nghe điện thoại She is
examples.en: texting while she is making a call (Actions at present time)

sections.heading: KHI…. / …KHI… – WHEN
sections.explanation: To express two actions done at the same time by one or more than one person or one subject, you can use “khi” by following this 1 + khi + subject + action 2 PATTERN #2 Khi + subject + action 1, subject + action 2 For.

examples.en: running, the dog barked The dog barks when I go running

examples.en: cooking When mom cooks, I am home from work

examples.vi: Trời mưa khi tôi đang học bài Khi tôi đang học bài, trời mưa It
examples.en: starts to rain when I am studying When I am studying, it starts to rain Khi ba xem TV,

examples.vi: mẹ tôi nấu ăn Khi mẹ tôi nấu ăn, ba tôi xem TV When dad
examples.en: watches TV, mom cooks When mom cooks, dad watches TV Em

examples.vi: ấy ngủ khi tôi đang nói chuyện Tôi nói chuyện khi em ấy đang ngủ She
examples.en: sleeps when I am talking I talk when she is sleeping Khi em

examples.vi: ấy học Tiếng Anh, tôi học Tiếng Hàn Khi tôi học Tiếng Hàn, em ấy học Tiếng Anh When she
examples.en: studies English, I study Korean When I study Korean, she studies English

faqs.question: Tôi vừa đi học vừa đi làm I both study and work Tôi vừa ăn cơm vừa xem TV I watch TV when I have rice How to use khi in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express two actions done at the same time by one or more than one person or one subject, you can use “khi” by following this pattern below: Subject + action 1 + khi + subject + action 2 or Khi + subject + action 1, subject + action 2 You can use “khi” only to describe two short actions which are happening at the same time or as a daily habit.

faqs.question: How to say “ very, too ” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, in order to express “very” or “too”, you can follow this pattern below: Rất + adjective! Rất nóng! → Việt Nam rất nóng! Too hot! → Vietnam is too hot!

faqs.question: What are common units of measurement?
faqs.answer: Common units include mét (meter), kilômet (kilometer), gam (gram), kilôgam (kilogram), and lít (liter).

faqs.question: How do you express length in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Length is typically expressed using units such as mét (meter) or centimét (centimeter), for example, “Chiều dài là hai mét” (The length is two meters).

faqs.question: What difference between trọng lượng & khối lượng?
faqs.answer: Trọng lượng refers to weight (the force exerted by gravity on an object), while khối lượng refers to mass (the amount of matter in an object).

faqs.question: Are there any measurement phrases unique to Vietnamese culture?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vietnamese culture uses specific measurements for things like rice or fabric, such as mấy chục cân (how many dozens of kilograms) or một thước (one measure, often referring to fabric length).

items.title: Adjective Comparisons in Vietnamese
items.pattern: Subject 1 + adjective + chẳng kém gì + Subject 2

example.vi: Anh ấy thông minh chẳng kém gì tôi

sections.heading: ADVANCED ADJECTIVE COMPARISONS IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, we explore advanced adjective comparisons in Vietnamese , going beyond basic structures like “hơn” and “nhất”. You’ll learn how to express subtle differences, similarities, and intensities using more nuanced vocabulary and sentence patterns.

examples.vi: “hơn” and “nhất” . You’ll
examples.en: learn how to express subtle differences, similarities, and intensities using more nuanced vocabulary and sentence patterns.

sections.heading: CHẲNG KÉM GÌ = NO LESS THAN / JUST AS…AS…
sections.explanation: This structure is used to express equality or to indicate that something is as good or impressive as another thing. It’s often used to show that two things are on the same level. For.
sections.pattern: Subject 1 + adjective + chẳng kém gì + Subject 2

sections.heading: CÒN HƠN CẢ= MORE THAN
sections.explanation: This structure emphasizes that something exceeds expectations or surpasses the comparison in some way, often indicating a degree that goes beyond what was expected. For.
