faqs.question: What are some advanced structures to compare adjectives in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Advanced structures include “so với,” “hơn nhiều,” “ít hơn,” “kém,” and “bằng nhau về mặt…” to express more precise or nuanced comparisons.

faqs.question: Can I use multiple adjectives in one comparison sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can use multiple adjectives by separating them with “và” (and) or structuring the sentence to highlight contrast, such as: “Anh ấy thông minh hơn và nhanh nhẹn hơn tôi.”

faqs.question: How do I express equality using adjectives?
faqs.answer: Use “cũng…như,” “giống như,” or “bằng nhau” with the adjective, such as “Cô ấy đẹp cũng như duyên dáng.”

faqs.question: What is the difference between “hơn” and “kém” in comparisons?
faqs.answer: “Hơn” is used when someone or something has more of a quality (e.g., cao hơn = taller), while “kém” expresses a lack of a quality compared to another (e.g., kém thông minh = less intelligent).

faqs.question: Are there formal vs informal ways to compare adjectives?
faqs.answer: Yes. Phrases like “so với” or “có vẻ như” are more formal, while “hơn,” “kém,” or “giống như” are commonly used in daily conversation.

extracted_patterns: Subject 1 + adjective + còn hơn cả + Subject 2

example.vi: Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY WITH IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Welcome to a beginner lesson on how to say “with” in Vietnamese. We’ll teach you various ways to express “with” in Vietnamese, with multiple to help you understand how to form sentences.

sections.heading: WITH IN VIETNAMESE WITH VỚI
sections.explanation: Với has exactly the same meaning of “with” in English. It can be used as follow:
sections.pattern: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi I come home with my mother Cô ấy ăn tối với chồng She has dinner with her husband Anh ấy đến trường với vợ He goes to school with his wife

examples.vi: Chúng tôi sống với gia đình We live with our
Tôi thích học tiếng Anh với cô giáo tôi I like to
Tôi không thích nghe nhạc với bạn I
examples.en: don’t like listen to music with my friend

sections.heading: WITH IN VIETNAMESE WITH CÙNG
sections.explanation: Besides “với, you can also use “cùng” to express someone “with” someone, something “with” something:
sections.pattern: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi I come home with my mother Cô ấy ăn tối cùng chồng She has dinner with her husband Anh ấy đến trường cùng vợ He goes to school with his wife

examples.vi: Chúng tôi sống cùng gia đình We live with our
Tôi thích học tiếng Anh cùng cô giáo tôi I like to
Tôi không thích nghe nhạc cùng bạn I
examples.en: don’t like listen to music with my friend

sections.heading: TOGETHER WITH VỚI…CÙNG NHAU
sections.explanation: When you want to express “together”, this is how you can speak in Vietnamese:
sections.pattern: Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau I come home together with my mom Cô ấy với chồng ăn tối cùng nhau She has dinner together with her husband Anh ấy với vợ đến trường cùng nhau He goes to school together with his wife

examples.vi: Chúng tôi với gia đình sống cùng nhau We live
Tôi với cô giáo thích học tiếng Anh cùng nhau My
examples.en: teacher and I like to study English together

examples.vi: Tôi vớ i bạn tôi không thích nghe nhạc cùng nhau My
examples.en: friend and I don’t like listen to music together

examples.vi: với ai? / cùng ai?
Với ai? Với mẹ tôi With who? With my mom Cô ấy ăn tối với ai? Cô ấy ăn tối với chồng Who does she have
examples.en: dinner with? She has dinner together with her husband Anh

examples.vi: ấy đến trường cùng ai? Anh ấy đến trường cùng vợ He goes to
examples.en: school with who? He goes to school together with his wife

examples.vi: Cùng ai? Cùng nhau With

faqs.question: How to use với ?
faqs.answer: Với has exactly the same meaning of “with” in English. It can be used as follow: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Cô ấy ăn tối với chồng . She has dinner with her husband Anh ấy đến trường với vợ.

faqs.question: He goes to school with his wife How to use cùng ?
faqs.answer: Besides “với, you can also use “cùng” to express someone “with” someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Cô ấy ăn tối cùng chồng . She has dinner with her husband Anh ấy đến trường cùng vợ.

faqs.question: He goes to school with his wife How to use với … cùng nhau ?
faqs.answer: When you want to express “together”, this is how you can speak in Vietnamese: Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau . I come home together with my mom Cô ấy với chồng ăn tối cùng nhau . She has dinner together with her husband Anh ấy với vợ đến trường cùng nhau .

faqs.question: He goes to school together with his wife How to say with in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: With in Vietnamese can be said by using multiple words. The most common sentence structures are: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau .

items.title: How to say "or" in Vietnamese
items.pattern: N1/ V1/ A1 + hoặc + N2/ V2/ A2

example.vi: Trái cam hoặc trái táo

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY OR IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Choices are part of daily life conversations, such as “would you like to stay home or go for a walk?” We teach you how to give options and choices by saying “or” in Vietnamese .

sections.heading: USING HOẶC
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese you will use the word “hoặc” to give more than two options in a sentence. It can be used with nouns, verbs and adjectives. PATTERN N1/ V1/ A1 + hoặc + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hoặc + N3/ V3/ A3) Trái cam hoặc trái táo Orange or Apple Cái bàn hoặc cái ghế Table or chair.

examples.vi: Đi học hoặc đi du lịch Going to
Ăn cơm hoặc uống nước Eating rice or
Đẹp hoặc xấu hoặc lạ Pretty or ugly or
hoặc dở Delicious or

sections.heading: USING HAY
sections.explanation: Another way to say “or” in Vietnamese is with the word hay. Just as before, it is used between nouns, verbs and adjectives. The two words hay and hoặc can be use interchangeably and equally in any situation. PATTERN N1/ V1/ A1 + hay + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hay + N3/ V3/ A3) Ba hay Mẹ Dad or mom Con gái hay con trai Girl or boy?.

examples.vi: Làm việc hay học bài Work or Study? Ngủ hay ăn Sleep or Eat? Vui hay buồn Happy or Sad? Cứng hay mềm Tough or

sections.heading: HOẶC & HAY IN QUESTIONS
sections.explanation: Now that we know how to use or in Vietnamese, let’s see how to use hoặc and hay in questions: Em muốn ăn táo hay ăn cam? Do you want to eat apples or eat oranges? Em yêu ai , ba hay mẹ? Who do you love, dad or mom? Em học giỏi môn nào , Toán hay Văn? Which subject are you good at, Maths or Literature? Chị muốn kem vị gì , cam hoặc bưởi? Which ice cream flavor do you want, orange or grapefruit? Chị muốn xem phim nào , Marvel hoặc Inside out? Which movie do you want to watch, Marvel or Inside out? Cuối tuần chị đi đâu , Việt Nam hoặc Mỹ ? Where do you want to go at the weekend, Vietnam or America?.

faqs.question: How to say or in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Or in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words hoặc and hay .

faqs.question: How to use hoặc in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese you will use the word “hoặc” to give more than two options in a sentence .

faqs.question: How to say with in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: With in Vietnamese can be said by using multiple words. The most common sentence structures are: Subject 1 + Verb + với + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà với mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother someone, something “with” something: Subject 1 + Verb + cùng + Subject 2 Tôi về nhà cùng mẹ tôi. I come home with my mother Subject 1 + với (with) + Subject 2 + verb + cùng nhau (together) Tôi với mẹ về nhà cùng nhau . I come home together with my mom — Learn more about how to say with in Vietnamese here.

example.vi: Tôi hầu như đọc tất cả cuốn sách ở đây

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY ALMOST IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you will learn how to express the concept of “almost” in Vietnamese by using the terms “gần như” and “hầu như.” These phrases are essential for describing situations that are nearly complete or almost true.

examples.vi: “gần như” and “hầu như.” These
examples.en: phrases are essential for describing situations that are nearly complete or almost true.

examples.vi: Tôi hầu như đọc tất cả cuốn sách ở đây I
examples.en: almost read all the books here The action of “reading” is measured by the number of books

examples.vi: Tôi hầu như không ăn táo I
examples.en: almost not eat any apples The action of “not eating” is measured by the number of apples notes Anh

examples.vi: ấy hầu như không ăn sáng mỗi buổi sáng He
examples.en: almost doesn’t eat breakfast every morning The action of “not eating breakfast” is repeated in every morning

examples.vi: Chị ấy hầu như đi ngủ lúc 8h00 mỗi buổi tối She
examples.en: almost goes to bed at 8pm every night The action of “going to bed is repeated in every night

examples.vi: Chúng tôi hầu như đi làm mỗi ngày We
examples.en: almost go to work everyday The action of “going to work” is repeated in every day

examples.vi: Họ hầu như không ở nhà thứ 7 They are
examples.en: almost not at home on Saturday The action of “not being at home” is repeated on every Saturday

sections.heading: USING GẦN NHƯ
sections.explanation: To express “almost” in Vietnamese you can also use gần như , more specifically when you almost do something (nearly 90%) at once or at one specific time/moment .
sections.pattern: Subject + gần như + verb Em gần như quên anh rồi! (Now) I can almost forget you! Tôi gần như trở thành 1 người khác I almost become another person (at this moment)

Tôi gần như không ngủ đêm qua I
examples.en: could not almost sleep last night

faqs.question: How to say almost in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Almost in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words gần như and hầu như .

faqs.question: How to use hầu như ?
faqs.answer: You will use “Hầu như” to express when you almost do something (nearly 90%) which is repeated or can be measured physically such as by quantity, by meters, by time, by days and months .

faqs.question: Subject + hầu như + verb Tôi hầu như đọc tất cả cuốn sách ở đây I almost read all the books here How to use gần như ?
faqs.answer: Another expression for “almost” can be used when you almost do something (nearly 90%) at once or at one specific time/moment!

faqs.question: Subject + gần như + verb Tôi gần như trở thành 1 người khác I almost become another person (at this moment) How to say or in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Or in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words hoặc and hay . In Vietnamese you will use the word “hoặc” to give more than two options in a sentence . N1/ V1/ A1 + hoặc + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hoặc + N3/ V3/ A3) Cái bàn hoặc cái ghế Table or chair Another way to say “or” in Vietnamese is with the word hay . It is used between nouns, verbs and adjectives. The two words hay and hoặc can be use interchangeably and equally in any situation.

faqs.question: N1/ V1/ A1 + hay + N2/ V2/ A2 (+ hay + N3/ V3/ A3) Con gái hay con trai Girl or boy?
faqs.answer: — Learn more about how to say or in Vietnamese here.

example.vi: Nếu anh không đến được, nhớ báo em nhá!

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY IF IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: This lesson will cover the most common way to say if in Vietnamese using Nếu and Nếu như. We will also have a look at how to negate this word to form if not in Vietnamese .

examples.vi: Nếu and Nếu như . We will also have a look at how to
examples.en: negate this word to form if not in Vietnamese .

sections.heading: USING NẾU / NẾU NHƯ
sections.explanation: To express if in Vietnamese you’ll need to use the words Nếu or Nếu như.

examples.vi: Nếu anh không đến được, nhớ báo em nhá! If you
Nếu không ăn sáng, tôi sẽ bị đau bụng If i

sections.heading: USING NẾU / NẾU NHƯ IN THE COMMAND FORM
sections.explanation: You can also place Nếu / Nếu như at the middle of sentence when you want to command someone. 1, nếu/nếu như + Clause 2 Đi theo anh, nếu em thích Follow me, if you like! Làm việc đi, nếu muốn có tiền Go to work, if you want money!.

sections.explanation: To turn it into the negative form, do not add any subjects following “Nếu không”.

faqs.question: How to say if in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express if in Vietnamese you’ll need to use the words Nếu or Nếu như . Nếu/ Nếu như + Clause 1, Clause 2 Nếu anh không đến được, nhớ báo em nhá! If you can’t come, let me know!

faqs.question: How to say if not in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: If not in Vietnamese is Nếu không .

faqs.question: How to say or in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Or in Vietnamese can be expressed by using the words hoặc and hay . Learn more about how to say or in Vietnamese here.

example.vi: Mẹ cùng ba đi du lịch

sections.heading: SAYING TOGETHER IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing “together” uses specific words and structures to convey joint actions or shared experiences.

examples.vi: “cùng” , “cùng

sections.heading: CÙNG
sections.explanation: “Cùng” is a straightforward way to say “together.” It’s commonly paired with verbs to show people are doing something together.

examples.vi: Tôi cùng anh ấy làm bài tập I and him do
Cô ấy cùng bạn học nhạc She and her
cùng anh băng qua đại dương I and you
Mẹ cùng ba đi du lịch Mom and dad go
Chúng tôi cùng học bài We
Cả lớp tôi cùng ăn trưa Our

sections.heading: VỚI NHAU
sections.explanation: “Với nhau” is an expression that emphasizes the reciprocity in the action. Together, but with each other. For.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi ăn tối với nhau We have
Họ nói chuyện với nhau hàng ngày. They talk to each
Nhóm bạn làm việc với nhau Friends work with each
Chúng tôi tâm sự với nhau thường xuyên We
Họ chia sẻ cảm xúc với nhau They
Họ thích hát với nhau They like to sing with each

sections.heading: CÙNG NHAU
sections.explanation: “Cùng nhau” is an expression that highlights the group nature of the activity. For.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi cùng nhau chuẩn bị bữa tối We
Họ cùng nhau tập thể dục buổi sáng They
examples.en: exercise together in the morning. Gia

examples.vi: đình cùng nhau l àm vườn The
Chúng tôi cùng nhau giải quyết vấn đề We
Bạn bè cùng nhau tổ chức sự kiện Friends
Nhóm bạn cùng nhau xem bóng đá A
examples.en: group of friends watch soccer together .

faqs.question: How do you say “together” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The word “cùng nhau” is commonly used to mean “together” in Vietnamese.

faqs.question: Can “cùng nhau” formally and informally?
faqs.answer: Yes, “cùng nhau” is versatile and can be used in both formal and informal contexts.

faqs.question: Where do I place “cùng nhau” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Cùng nhau” is usually placed before the verb, as in “Chúng ta cùng nhau học tiếng Việt” (We study Vietnamese together).

faqs.question: Is “cùng” alone enough to mean “together”?
faqs.answer: Yes, “cùng” alone can imply “together” and is often used in casual speech. However, “cùng nhau” gives more emphasis to the idea of doing something collectively.

faqs.question: Can “cùng nhau” be used with nouns?
faqs.answer: “Cùng nhau” is primarily used with verbs to describe people doing something together, rather than with nouns.

items.title: How to say "in order to" in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi học tiếng Việt để làm việc với người Việt hiệu quả

sections.heading: SAYING IN ORDER TO IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, the phrase “in order to” is expressed using specific particles and verbs that indicate purpose. The most common terms used are “để,” “để cho,” and “nhằm.”.

sections.heading: HOW TO USE ĐỂ
sections.explanation: “Để” is the most common way to express purpose and is used similarly to “in order to.” For.

examples.vi: Tôi học tiếng Việt để làm việc với người Việt hiệu quả In
examples.en: order to work with Vietnamese effectively, I learn Vietnamese

examples.vi: Cô ấy tập thể dục mỗi ngày để giữ sức khỏe. In
examples.en: order to stay healthy, she exercises everyday

examples.vi: Họ tiết kiệm tiền từ bây giờ để mua nhà
năm nữa. In
examples.en: order to buy a house in next 10 years, they start saving up money from now

examples.vi: Họ học từ vựng để nói trôi chảy Tiếng Anh. In
examples.en: order to speak English fluently, they study vocabulary

examples.vi: Tôi chạy bộ để giảm cân In
examples.en: order to lose weight, I run

examples.vi: Tôi làm việc chăm chỉ để có nhiều tiền In
examples.en: order to earn a lot of money, I word hard

sections.heading: HOW TO USE NHẰM
sections.explanation: “Nhằm” conveys a more formal or intentional purpose. PATTERN #1 Subject + Verb + nhằm + Verb PATTERN #2 Nhằm + Verb, Subject + Verb For.

examples.vi: Chúng tôi làm việc chăm chỉ nhằm cải thiện chất lượng cuộc sống We word hard in
examples.en: order to improve the quality of life

examples.vi: Nhằm cải thiện chất lượng cuộc sống, chúng tôi làm việc chăm chỉ In
examples.en: order to improve the quality of life, we work hard

examples.vi: Vứt rác vào thùng rác nhằm bảo vệ môi trường Put
examples.en: trash into the bin in order to save the environment

examples.vi: Nhằm bảo vệ môi trường, xin đừng chặt cây In
examples.en: order to save the environment, please do not cut down the trees Ba

examples.vi: tôi quyên góp tiền nhằm giúp người nghèo My
examples.en: father makes a donation in order to help the poor

examples.vi: Nhằm giúp người nghèo, ba tôi kêu gọi quyên góp từ cộng đồng In
examples.en: order to help the poor, my father calls for a donation from community

faqs.question: How do you say “in order to” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The phrase “để” is commonly used to express “in order to” in Vietnamese.

faqs.question: Where do I place “để” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Để” is typically placed before the verb that follows the purpose, as in “Tôi học tiếng Việt để nói chuyện với bạn bè” (I study Vietnamese in order to talk with friends).

faqs.question: Can “để” be used for formal and informal?
faqs.answer: Yes, “để” is versatile and appropriate in both formal and informal contexts.

faqs.question: Is “để” only used with verbs?
faqs.answer: Primarily, yes. “Để” is used with verbs to indicate purpose, like “để học” (to study) or “để làm việc” (to work).

items.title: How to say "this" and "that" in Vietnamese

example.vi: Cái này là món ăn tôi thích nhất

sections.heading: THIS & THAT IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, “this” and “that” can be expressed as “này” and “đó” respectively. Understanding how to use these expressions can enhance your communication skills.

examples.vi: “này” and “đó”
examples.en: respectively. Understanding how to use these expressions can enhance your communication skills.

sections.heading: CÁI NÀY (LÀ)…= THIS (IS)…
sections.explanation: “Cái này (là)…” means “This (is)…” and is used with the sentence structure below: PATTERN Cái này + (là) + Noun/ Adjective/Verb/Clause For.

examples.vi: Cái này là món ăn tôi thích nhất This is my
Cái này là cuốn sách tôi đã đọc This is the book I have read Cái này rất đẹp This is very
Cái này rất dễ sử dụng This is very easy to use Cái này không hợp với tôi This
Cái này có hương vị ngon This has a
“Cái đó (là)…”
examples.en: means “That (is)…” and is used with the structure below: PATTERN

examples.vi: Cái đó + (là) + Noun/ Adjective/Verb/Clause Cái đó là món ăn tôi thích nhất That is my
Cái đó là cuốn sách tôi đã đọc That is the book I have read Cái đó rất đẹp That is very
Cái đó rất dễ sử dụng That is very easy to use Cái đó không hợp với tôi That
Cái đó có hương vị ngon That has a

sections.heading: …NÀY= THIS…
sections.explanation: In order to say “this book, this pen, this paper, etc”, please use the pattern below: PATTERN Noun + này Cuốn sách này rất hay This book is very good Bức tranh này được vẽ bởi cô ấy. This painting was painted by her Cái áo này màu xanh This shirt is blue Bánh mì này rất ngon This bread is very delicious. Kế hoạch này cần thay đổi This plan needs to be changed Bộ phim này được đánh giá cao This movie is highly rated.

examples.vi: này Cuốn sách này rất hay This book is very good Bức
này được vẽ bởi cô ấy. This
Cái áo này màu xanh This
Bánh mì này rất ngon This
Kế hoạch này cần thay đổi This plan
Bộ phim này được đánh giá cao This

sections.heading: …ĐÓ = THAT…
sections.explanation: In order to say “that book,that pen, that paper, etc”, you can use the following sentence structure: PATTERN Noun + đó Cuốn sách đó rất hay That book is very good Bức tranh đó được vẽ bởi cô ấy. That painting was painted by her Cái áo đó màu xanh That shirt is blue Bánh mì đó rất ngon That bread is very delicious. Kế hoạch đó cần thay đổi That plan needs to be changed Bộ phim đó được đánh giá cao That movie is highly rated.

examples.vi: đó Cuốn sách đó rất hay That book is very good Bức
đó được vẽ bởi cô ấy. That
Cái áo đó màu xanh That
Bánh mì đó rất ngon That
Kế hoạch đó cần thay đổi That plan
Bộ phim đó được đánh giá cao That

faqs.question: How do I say this and that in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, “this” is typically translated as này , and “that” as kia or đó , depending on context and distance.

faqs.question: How to say only in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Only in Vietnamese can be expressed using the words chỉ and mỗi , as well as both words under the expression chỉ…mỗi… . Subject + chỉ + verb/adjective Mỗi (only) + noun (except for time and date) Chỉ + verb + mỗi + noun (except for time and date) — Learn more about only in Vietnamese here.

faqs.question: How to say if in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To express if in Vietnamese you’ll need to use the words Nếu or Nếu như. Nếu/ Nếu như + Clause 1, Clause 2 Nếu anh không đến được, nhớ báo em nhá! If you can’t come, let me know! — Learn more about how to say if in Vietnamese here.

items.title: How to say "after all" in Vietnamese

example.vi: Cuối cùng, tôi đã quyết định không đi

sections.heading: AFTER ALL IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: We often find ourselves reflecting on decisions and outcomes. The phrase “sau cùng” or “cuối cùng” in Vietnamese allows us to articulate those moments of realization or conclusions that come after careful thought.

examples.vi: cùng” or “cuối cùng” in Vietnamese
examples.en: allows us to articulate those moments of realization or conclusions that come after careful thought.

sections.heading: CUỐI CÙNG…
sections.explanation: Cuối cùng is an adverb frequently used in both spoken and written Vietnamese to describe the final outcome of a process , the last event in a sequence , or a decision reached after consideration. PATTERN Cuối cùng, Subject + Verb For.

examples.vi: Cuối cùng, tôi đã quyết định không đi After all , I
Cuối cùng, em vẫn phải làm bài tập After all , you
examples.en: still have to do your homework

examples.vi: Cuối cùng , anh vẫn bị mất tiền After all , he
Cuối cùng , ba đã hết giận After all , Dad is no
Cuối cùng , tôi cũng ngủ được bình thường After all , I can
Cuối cùng , tôi đã có chồng After all , I got

sections.heading: SAU CÙNG…
sections.explanation: This is an adverb to state “ after all ” which is same as “ cuối cùng ” but sounds more formal and it is usually used as written Vietnamese. PATTERN Sau cùng, Subject + Verb For.

examples.vi: cùng, tôi đã quyết định không đi After all , I
examples.en: finally decided not to go Sau

examples.vi: cùng , chúng tôi vẫn yêu nhau After all , we
cùng , người chiến thắng trái tim tôi vẫn là cô ấy After all , the
examples.en: winner of my heart is still her Sau

examples.vi: cùng , người thất bại vẫn là tôi After all , I am
cùng , tôi vẫn yêu em After all , I
cùng , em vẫn là người sai After all , you are

faqs.question: How do you say “after all” in Vietnamese to indicate a conclusion?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, “sau tất cả” or “rốt cuộc” can be used to mean “after all” when summing up or reaching a conclusion.

faqs.question: Can “after all” expressions be used in both positive and negative sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes, terms like “sau tất cả” or “rốt cuộc” are neutral and can be used in sentences with both positive and negative meanings.

faqs.question: How can I use “after all” to add emphasis in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: To add emphasis, you can use “sau cùng thì” to stress that an action or outcome was expected all along.

faqs.question: Can I use “sau tất cả” in formal and informal settings?
faqs.answer: Yes, “sau tất cả” is versatile and can be used in both formal and informal settings to express the idea of “after all.”

example.vi: Tôi rất thích đọc sách. Hơn nữa, tôi thích xem phim.

sections.heading: MOREOVER IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Moreover in Vietnamese can be expressed in two ways. When you want to add information, reinforce points, and enhance their arguments, you can use the phrases “hơn nữa” and “ngoài ra”.

examples.vi: Tôi rất thích đọc sách. Hơn nữa , tôi thích xem
examples.en: phim. I really like reading books. Moreover , I enjoy watching movies

examples.vi: Cô ấy là một học sinh xuất sắc. Hơn nữa , cô ấy cũng là một học sinh tham gia nhiều hoạt động ngoại khóa. She is an
examples.en: excellent student. Moreover , she is also the student who participates in many extracurricular activities

examples.vi: Họ đã hoàn thành dự án đúng hạn. Hơn nữa , họ đã nhận được giải thưởng They
examples.en: completed the project on time. Moreover, they received an award Anh

examples.vi: ấy rất thông
Hơn nữa , anh ấy còn rất chăm chỉ He is very
examples.en: intelligent. Moreover, he is also very hardworking

examples.vi: Cô ấy nói tiếng Anh rất tốt. Hơn nữa , cô ấy còn nói tiếng Pháp She
examples.en: speaks English very well. Moreover , she also speaks French

examples.vi: Tôi rất thích cà phê. Ngoài ra , tôi còn thích trà I
examples.en: really like coffee. In addition , I also enjoy tea

examples.vi: Học sinh cần làm bài tập về nhà. Ngoài ra, họ nên đọc thêm sách để mở rộng kiến thức. Students need to do
examples.en: their homework. Besides , they should read more books to broaden their knowledge

examples.vi: Cô ấy rất giỏi nấu ăn. Ngoài ra , cô ấy cũng
trí nhà rất đẹp She is very good at
examples.en: cooking. Moreover , she also decorates the house very beautifully

examples.vi: Đi bộ hàng ngày rất tốt cho sức khỏe. Ngoài ra, nó cũng giúp giảm
examples.en: stress. Walking daily is very good for your health. In addition , it also helps reduce stress.

examples.vi: Món ăn này rất
Ngoài ra , giá cả cũng rất hợp lý. This dish is very
examples.en: delicious. Besides , the prices are also very reasonable

examples.vi: ấy làm việc rất chăm chỉ. Thêm vào đó , anh ấy luôn đúng giờ. He
examples.en: works very hard. In addition, he is always punctual

examples.vi: Cô giáo giảng bài rất dễ hiểu. Thêm vào đó , cô ấy rất kiên nhẫn với học
examples.en: sinh. The teacher explains lessons very clearly. In addition, she is very patient with students.

examples.vi: Quán cà phê này có không gian đẹp. Thêm vào đó , đồ uống rất
café has a
examples.en: beautiful space. In addition, the drinks are delicious. Anh

examples.vi: ấy tập thể dục mỗi ngày. Thêm vào đó , anh ấy còn ăn uống lành mạnh. He
examples.en: exercises every day. In addition, he also eats healthily.

examples.vi: Cô ấy nói tiếng Anh rất tốt. Thêm vào đó , cô ấy còn nói tiếng Pháp She
examples.en: speaks English very well. In addition, she also speaks French

faqs.question: How to say moreover in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The word “hơn nữa” is commonly used to express “moreover” or “in addition” in Vietnamese, providing a way to add further information or reasons in a sentence.

faqs.question: Can hơn nữa be used at the beginning?
faqs.answer: Yes, “hơn nữa” can be used at the beginning of a sentence or clause to emphasize additional information, much like starting with “moreover” in English.

faqs.question: Any other words similar to hơn nữa ?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can also use “ngoài ra” (besides/aside from that) or “thêm vào đó” (in addition) for a similar meaning, although each has a slightly different nuance.

faqs.question: Does hơn nữa require a specific structure?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. “Hơn nữa” is ble and can be added between clauses, after a pause, or at the start of a sentence to add information without changing sentence structure.

faqs.question: Is hơn nữa formal or informal?
faqs.answer: “Hơn nữa” is neutral and can be used in both formal and informal contexts, making it versatile for everyday conversations as well as written communication.

example.vi: Tôi sẽ chờ bạn cho đến khi bạn đến

sections.heading: UNTIL IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, the word “cho đến khi”, “đến khi”, “tới khi” are used to indicate a point in time up to which an action continues. Understanding how to use those expressions effectively will help enhance your conversations.

examples.vi: “tới khi” are used to
examples.en: indicate a point in time up to which an action continues. Understanding how to use those expressions effectively will help enhance your conversations.

sections.heading: CHO ĐẾN KHI…
sections.explanation: “Cho đến khi” means “until” or “up until the point when.” It sounds slightly formal and emphasizes the continuation of an action or state up to a specific moment or condition.

examples.vi: đến khi”
examples.en: means “until” or “up until the point when.” It sounds slightly formal and emphasizes the continuation of an action or state up to a specific moment or condition. 1 + cho

examples.vi: đến khi + Clause 2 Tôi sẽ chờ bạn cho đến khi bạn đến I will wait
Họ sẽ làm việc, cho đến khi dự án hoàn thành They will work
Cô ấy học chăm chỉ, cho đến khi thi xong She
examples.en: studies hard until the exam is over

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ ở đây, cho đến khi trời tối We will stay here
Tôi sẽ đọc sách, cho đến khi cảm thấy buồn ngủ I will read a book
Chúng ta sẽ không rời đi, cho đến khi mọi người đến đủ We will not
Tôi sẽ làm bài tập, cho đến khi hoàn thành tất cả I will do my
Chúng ta sẽ không rời khỏi nhà, cho đến khi trời sáng We will not
examples.en: leave the house until it gets light Anh

examples.vi: ấy sẽ ăn kiêng, cho đến khi giảm được cân He will go on a diet
Tôi sẽ không ngủ, cho đến khi xem hết phim I will not
examples.en: sleep until I finish watching the movie

sections.heading: ĐẾN KHI…
sections.explanation: “Cho đến khi” means “until” or “up until the point when.” It sounds slightly formal and emphasizes the continuation of an action or state up to a specific moment or condition.

examples.en: means “until” or “up until the point when.” It sounds slightly formal and emphasizes the continuation of an action or state up to a specific moment or condition. 1 + cho

Họ sẽ làm việc, cho đến khi dự án hoàn thành They will work
Cô ấy học chăm chỉ, cho đến khi thi xong She

Tôi sẽ không ngủ, cho đến khi xem hết phim I will not
examples.en: sleep until I finish watching the movie

examples.vi: ĐẾN KHI… “Đến khi” is
đến khi + Clause 2 Tôi sẽ chờ bạn đến khi bạn đến I will wait
Họ sẽ làm việc, đến khi dự án hoàn thành They will work
Cô ấy học chăm chỉ, đến khi thi xong She
examples.en: studies hard until the exam is over

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ ở đây, đến khi trời tối We will stay here
Tôi sẽ đọc sách, đến khi cảm thấy buồn ngủ I will read a book
Chúng ta sẽ không rời đi, đến khi mọi người đến đủ We will not
Tôi sẽ làm bài tập, đến khi hoàn thành tất cả I will do my
Chúng ta sẽ không rời khỏi nhà, đến khi trời sáng We will not
examples.en: leave the house until it gets light Anh

Tôi sẽ không ngủ, đến khi xem hết phim I will not
examples.en: sleep until I finish watching the movie

sections.heading: TỚI KHI…
sections.explanation: “ Tới khi ” also means “until,” and it indicates an endpoint for an action, often in a more casual, conversational tone.

examples.vi: Tới khi ” also
examples.en: means “until,” and it indicates an endpoint for an action, often in a more casual, conversational tone. 1 +

examples.vi: tới khi + Clause 2 Tôi sẽ chờ bạn tới khi bạn đến I will wait
Họ sẽ làm việc, tới khi dự án hoàn thành They will work
Cô ấy học chăm chỉ, tới khi thi xong She
examples.en: studies hard until the exam is over

examples.vi: Chúng ta sẽ ở đây, tới khi trời tối We will stay here
Tôi sẽ đọc sách, tới khi cảm thấy buồn ngủ I will read a book
Chúng ta sẽ không rời đi, tới khi mọi người đến đủ We will not
Tôi sẽ làm bài tập, tới khi hoàn thành tất cả I will do my
Chúng ta sẽ không rời khỏi nhà, tới khi trời sáng We will not

Tôi sẽ không ngủ, tới khi xem hết phim I will not
examples.en: sleep until I finish watching the movie

faqs.question: How to say until in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The word “đến khi” is commonly used to express “until” in Vietnamese, indicating the point at which an action or situation will continue.

faqs.question: Can “đến khi” be used at the beginning?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đến khi” can be used at the start of a sentence to set a condition or timeframe, such as in “Đến khi anh về, em sẽ ở đây” (“Until you come back, I will be here”).

faqs.question: Any difference between “tới khi” and “đến khi”?
faqs.answer: No significant difference; both “tới khi” and “đến khi” mean “until” and are interchangeable. “Đến khi” may be slightly more common in usage.

faqs.question: Are there other expressions similar to “đến khi”?
faqs.answer: Yes, “cho đến khi” or simply “cho đến” can also be used, especially in more formal or literary contexts.

example.vi: Tôi phải đi học

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY MUST IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Have to and must in Vietnamese are expressed using only one word: phải. We teach you how to use obligation in Vietnamese in the declarative, negative and interrogative form.

sections.heading: PHẢI || HAVE TO, MUST
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, there is only one word to express something that you must do or have to do. The word is “phải”. tiếng mỗi ngày She has to sleep 8 hours everyday.
sections.pattern: Subject + phải + verb Tôi phải đi học I have to go to school Anh ấy phải đi làm He has to go to work Chị ấy phải ngủ 8

examples.vi: Chúng tôi phải nghỉ ngơi We have to take a rest Em ấy phải ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối She has to have
phải học Tiếng Việt mỗi ngày You have to

sections.heading: NEGATIVE FORM
sections.pattern: Subject + không phải + verb Tôi không phải đi học I don’t have to go to school Anh ấy không phải đi làm He doesn’t have to go to work Chị ấy không phải ngủ 8

examples.vi: Chúng tôi không phải nghỉ ngơi We
examples.en: don’t have to take a rest Em

examples.vi: ấy không phải ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối She
examples.en: doesn’t have to have dinner at 7pm Anh

examples.vi: không phải học Tiếng Việt mỗi ngày You
examples.en: don’t have to learn Vietnamese everyday

sections.heading: PHẢI LÀM GÌ || WHAT DO I HAVE TO DO?
sections.explanation: This is the sentence structure to follow in order to make a question with “phải”: tiếng mỗi ngày What does she have to do everyday? She has to sleep 8 hours everyday.
sections.pattern: Subject + phải + làm gì? Bạn phải làm gì mỗi ngày? Tôi phải đi học mỗi ngày What do you have to do everyday? I have to go to school Anh ấy phải làm gì mỗi ngày? Anh ấy phải đi làm mỗi ngày What does he have to do everyday? He has to go to work Chị ấy phải làm gì mỗi ngày? Chị ấy phải ngủ 8

examples.vi: Bạn phải làm gì mỗi ngày? Chúng tôi phải nghỉ ngơi mỗi ngày What do you have to do
examples.en: everyday? We have to take a rest Em

examples.vi: ấy phải làm gì lúc 7h00 tối? Em ấy phải ăn cơm lúc 7h00 tối What does she have to do
examples.en: every 7pm? She has to have dinner at 7pm Anh

examples.vi: phải làm gì mỗi ngày? Anh phải học Tiếng Việt mỗi ngày What do I have to do
examples.en: everyday? You have to learn Vietnamese everyday

faqs.question: How to say must in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: In Vietnamese, there is only one word to express something that you must do or have to do. The word is “phải” . Subject + phải + verb Tôi phải đi học. I have to go to school Anh ấy phải đi làm.

faqs.question: He has to go to work How to say do not have to in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: You can add “không” before “phải” in order to turn the sentence in the negative form: Subject + không phải + verb Tôi không phải đi học. I don’t have to go to school Anh ấy không phải đi làm.

faqs.question: What do you have to do everyday?
faqs.answer: Tôi phải đi học mỗi ngày.

faqs.question: I have to go to school Is Vietnamese SVO or SOV?
faqs.answer: The Vietnamese language structure is SVO, meaning sentences are built according to Subject + Verb + Object. Learn more about the Vietnamese sentence structure here.

items.title: How to say "used to" in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi đã từng sống ở Hà Nội

sections.heading: HOW TO SAY “USED TO” IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing the idea of “used to” or past experiences is commonly done using structures like đã từng , từng , chưa từng , and chưa bao giờ. These expressions help convey whether someone has ever done something in the past or has never experienced it at all.

examples.vi: đã từng , từng , chưa từng , and chưa bao giờ . These
examples.en: expressions help convey whether someone has ever done something in the past or has never experienced it at all.

sections.heading: TỪNG / ĐÃ TỪNG
sections.explanation: The phrase từng/đã từng is used to express an action or state that happened in the past, but is no longer true or does not happen anymore. It corresponds to “ used to ” in English. For.

examples.vi: Tôi đã từng sống ở Hà Nội I used to live in Hanoi Cô ấy đã từng là bạn thân của tôi She used to be my best
Họ đã từng đi bơi mỗi cuối tuần They used to go
Chúng tôi đã từng ăn cùng nhau mỗi ngày We used to eat
Tôi đã từng thăm ông bà mỗi mùa hè I used to
examples.en: visit my grandparents every summer Anh

examples.vi: ấy đã từng làm việc tại một ngân hàng He used to work at a bank

sections.heading: CHƯA TỪNG
sections.explanation: This pattern is used to express the negative form of “used to” meaning “did not use to…yet”. For.

examples.vi: Tôi chưa từng sống ở Hà Nội I did not use to live in Hanoi Cô ấy chưa từng là bạn thân của tôi She did not use to be my best
Họ đã chưa đi bơi mỗi cuối tuần They did not use to go
Chúng tôi chưa từng ăn cùng nhau mỗi ngày We did not use to eat
Tôi chưa từng thăm ông bà mỗi mùa hè I did not use to
examples.en: visit my grandparents every summer Anh

sections.heading: ĐÃ TỪNG…CHƯA? / ĐÃ BAO GIỜ…CHƯA?
sections.explanation: This pattern is used to express the yes/no question of “ Did you use to…/Have you ever done…yet? ” PATTERN #1 Subject + đã từng/đã bao giờ + verb + chưa? PATTERN #2 Yes: Rồi, subject + đã từng + verb PATTERN #3 Not yet: Chưa, subject + chưa từng/chưa bao giờ + verb For.

examples.en: unemployed? -Yes, I used to be unemployed -No, I’ve not ever been unemployed Ba

examples.en: cook? -Yes, she used to cook for 5 people everyday -No, she’s never cooked yet

faqs.question: What is the difference between “đã từng” and “từng”?
faqs.answer: Both mean “used to” or “have ever,” but “đã từng” is slightly more formal and emphasizes the past experience, while “từng” is more neutral and commonly used in conversation.

faqs.question: Can I use “chưa từng” and “chưa bao giờ” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Yes , they both mean “have never,” but “chưa bao giờ” is often stronger and more emphatic. “Chưa từng” is more concise and slightly more formal.

faqs.question: Where should “từng” or “đã từng” be placed in a sentence?
faqs.answer: They typically appear before the main verb. For example: Tôi đã từng sống ở Hà Nội (“I used to live in Hanoi”).

faqs.question: Is “đã từng” always necessary to talk about past experiences?
faqs.answer: No , sometimes just using “đã” + verb is enough in context. “Đã từng” adds emphasis that the experience is now in the past and may not happen again.

faqs.question: Can I use these expressions in both positive and negative sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes . You can say Tôi đã từng đi Đà Nẵng (I used to go to Da Nang) or Tôi chưa từng đi Đà Nẵng (I have never been to Da Nang).

example.vi: Tôi sẽ thử làm chiếc bánh này

sections.heading: TRYING IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing the concept of “ try ” can be done using various structures depending on the context, such as attempting something or trying for a specific purpose. Let’s explore how to use “try” in different ways in Vietnamese!.

sections.heading: THỬ + VERB
sections.explanation: “ Thử ” is used when talking about trying something new or testing something. It suggests an attempt, often for the first time, to do something or experience something. PATTERN Thử + Verb For.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ thử làm chiếc bánh này I will try to make this cake Cô ấy muốn thử nói tiếng Anh She

sections.explanation: This structure is used to express the idea of t rying out an action or trying something with the intention of experiencing or testing it. It implies that the action is being attempted but not necessarily with high effort, and it is often for discovery or enjoyment. PATTERN Verb + Thử For.

examples.vi: Tôi sẽ làm thử chiếc bánh này I will try to make this cake Cô ấy muốn nói thử tiếng Anh She

sections.heading: CỐ GẮNG + VERB
sections.explanation: This is the most common way to express “ try ” in the sense of making an effort or attempting to do something. It implies putting in effort toward achieving a goal. PATTERN Cố gắng + Verb For.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “thử” and “cố gắng”?
faqs.answer: “Thử” means to try something out or test it, usually once or experimentally. “Cố gắng” means to make an effort or try hard to achieve something over time.

faqs.question: Can I use “thử” and “cố gắng” in the same sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can. For example: Tôi sẽ cố gắng thử làm món này (“I’ll try my best to give this dish a try”).

faqs.question: Where does “thử” go in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Thử” typically comes right before the verb it modifies. For example: Thử ăn món này đi! (“Try eating this dish!”).

faqs.question: Is “cố gắng” followed by any specific grammar structure?
faqs.answer: Yes , “cố gắng” is often followed by a verb or verb phrase, such as cố gắng học tiếng Việt (“try to study Vietnamese”).

faqs.question: Can “thử” be used in the past tense?
faqs.answer: Yes , you can use it with time indicators or context. For example: Tôi đã thử rồi (“I already tried it”).

example.vi: Em càng chạy, em càng đổ mồ hôi

sections.heading: THE MORE…THE MORE IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, we use certain patterns to express the idea of “the more… the more.” This structure highlights a correlation between two actions or states, often showing an increase in one thing as another increases.

sections.heading: CÀNG…CÀNG…
sections.explanation: This is the most common pattern for expressing “the more… the more.” It is used to indicate that as one thing increases, another thing increases as well.

sections.heading: STRUCTURE #1
sections.explanation: PATTERN Càng + [Verb], Càng + [Verb] For.

faqs.question: Can “càng…càng” be used with both verbs and adjectives?
faqs.answer: Yes , “càng…càng” can be used with both verbs and adjectives to express increasing intensity or correlation between two actions or states.

faqs.question: Is word order important when using “càng…càng”?
faqs.answer: Yes , the structure follows a clear pattern: càng + subject + verb/adjective, càng + subject + verb/adjective . Maintaining proper word order ensures clarity and correctness.

faqs.question: Can I omit the second subject in “càng…càng” sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes , if the subject remains the same in both clauses, you can omit the second one. For example: Càng học, càng giỏi.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “càng…càng” and “mỗi lúc một”?
faqs.answer: Both express intensification over time, but “càng…càng” highlights a correlation between two factors, while “mỗi lúc một” focuses on a single action or state increasing over time.

faqs.question: Can “càng…càng” be used in negative sentences?
faqs.answer: Yes , but it’s less common. You can express a decreasing trend or worsening condition, e.g., Càng nói, càng sai (“The more you speak, the more wrong you are”).

example.vi: Học bài cùng nhau nhé!

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE SUGGESTIONS

faqs.question: How to use nhé in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Adding “…nhé” at the end of a sentence softens the tone, making the suggestion sound friendly and inviting.

faqs.question: For example, “Chúng ta đi ăn nhé?
Let’s go eat, okay?
faqs.answer: ) feels gentle and polite.

faqs.question: How to use hay là in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Hay là…” is often used to offer an alternative or a suggestion.

faqs.question: For example, “Hay là chúng ta xem phim?
How about we watch a movie?
faqs.answer: ) allows for a choice and is casual and friendly.

faqs.question: How to use sao không in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Sao không…” (Why not…) suggests something that could or should be done, often to nudge someone toward an idea.

faqs.question: For instance, “Sao không đi dạo một chút?
Why not take a walk?
faqs.answer: ) gently encourages an action.

faqs.question: Is hay là suitable for both formal and informal?
faqs.answer: “Hay là…” is more casual and typically used in informal settings, like with friends or family. It may feel too familiar in formal contexts.

sections.heading: INTENTIONS IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing intentions is essential for discussing plans, goals, and desires. Key expressions such as định (intend), lên kế hoạch (making plans), and sẽ (will) allow speakers to communicate their intentions with clarity and nuance.

examples.vi: lên kế hoạch

faqs.question: How to use Định in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: “Định” means “to intend to” and is placed before a verb to indicate intention. For example, “Tôi định học tiếng Việt” (I intend to study Vietnamese).

faqs.question: What’s the difference between muốn and định ?
faqs.answer: “Muốn” expresses a desire (want) to do something, while “định” specifically indicates an intention or plan. For example, “Tôi muốn đi du lịch” (I want to travel) versus “Tôi định đi du lịch” (I intend to travel).

faqs.question: Can “sẽ” be used to express intention, or is it only for the future tense?
faqs.answer: “ Sẽ ” generally indicates a future action but can imply intent if the context is clear. However, “ định ” or “muốn” are more specific for intentional actions.

faqs.question: Can “đang định” be used to indicate an immediate intention?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đang định” implies an intention that you are actively considering or about to do soon. For example, “Tôi đang định gọi cho bạn” (I am about to call you).

example.vi: Anh ấy vừa thắng giải lớn thật à?

sections.heading: SURPRISE IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing surprise is often vivid and emotional, using short exclamations that convey shock, disbelief, or amazement. This lesson will introduce common phrases like Thật à , Không ngờ , and Trời ơi to help you sound more natural in everyday conversations.

sections.heading: THẬT Á? = REALLY??
sections.explanation: “ Thật à? ” is used to express astonishment upon hearing something unexpected or shocking! It’s similar to “ Really? ” or “ Is that true? ” in English. PATTERN Thật à? + Clause For.

Cô ấy có ba đứa con thật à? She has

sections.heading: TRỜI ƠI! = OMG!
sections.explanation: “ Trời ơi! ” expresses surprise, shock, or even exasperation. It’s equivalent to “ Oh my God! ” or “ Oh dear! ” in English and is widely used in informal contexts. PATTERN Trời ơi! + Clause For.

sections.heading: KHÔNG NGỜ = TO BE SURPRISED THAT…
sections.explanation: “Không ngờ” expresses that you are surprised at something unexpected. For.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “Thật à?
faqs.answer: ” and “

faqs.question: Thật không?
Both express surprise, but “Thật à?
faqs.answer: ” is more neutral/informal and used in spoken

faqs.question: Vietnamese, while “Thật không?
faqs.answer: ” can carry more disbelief or request for confirmation. Is “Trời ơi!” considered rude or inappropriate? No, “Trời ơi!” is very common and expressive in daily conversation, but tone and context matter. Use it carefully in formal settings.

faqs.question: Can “Không ngờ” be used at the beginning of a sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes , “Không ngờ…” (I didn’t expect…) often starts a sentence to show surprise or shock about an outcome.

faqs.question: Are there any formal ways to express surprise in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: While most surprise expressions are informal, phrases like “Tôi thật sự ngạc nhiên” (I’m truly surprised) are more formal or written.

faqs.question: Can I combine these expressions in one sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes, native speakers often layer expressions for emphasis, like “Trời ơi, không ngờ luôn đó!” , especially in spoken language.

example.vi: Thật tiếc là tôi không thể tham dự buổi tiệc.

sections.heading: REGRET IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, there are few phrases to express regret or a sense of sorrow, wishing things were different, or acknowledging a missed opportunity. This lesson will show you how to express regret in Vietnamese.

sections.heading: THẬT TIẾC LÀ = IT’S A PITY THAT…
sections.explanation: “Thật tiếc là” means “It’s a pity that” or “It’s unfortunate that.” It’s used to express disappointment about a situation that has happened or is currently happening. PATTERN Thật tiếc là + clause For.

examples.vi: Thật tiếc là tôi không thể tham dự buổi tiệc. It’s a pity that I
Thật tiếc là anh ấy không đồng ý với ý tưởng này. It’s
examples.en: unfortunate that he didn’t agree with this idea

examples.vi: Thật tiếc là chúng ta đã bỏ lỡ cơ hội đó. I t’s a pity that we
Thật tiếc là thời tiết hôm nay không đẹp. It’s
examples.en: unfortunate that the weather isn’t nice today

examples.vi: Thật tiếc là cô ấy đã từ chối lời mời của tôi. It’s a pity that she
Thật tiếc là tôi không có đủ thời
examples.en: gian. It’s unfortunate that I don’t have enough time

sections.heading: GIÁ NHƯ = IF ONLY
sections.explanation: “Giá như” expresses deep regret about unrealized scenarios, often referring to the past. PATTERN Giá như + claus e For.

examples.vi: Giá như tôi đã chăm chỉ hơn khi còn trẻ. If only I had
examples.en: worked harder when I was young

Giá như chúng ta đến sớm hơn một chút. If only we had

sections.heading: ĐÁNG LẼ RA = SHOULD HAVE
sections.explanation: “Đáng lẽ ra” means “should have” or “ought to have,” highlighting regret for a missed responsibility or action. PATTERN Đáng lẽ ra + Clause For.

examples.vi: Đáng lẽ ra tôi nên gọi cho anh ấy. I

sections.heading: BIẾT VẬY THÌ = HAD I KNOWN
sections.explanation: “Biết vậy thì” is used to express regret for a decision or action that could have been avoided For.

examples.vi: Biết vậy thì tôi đã không nói điều đó. If I had

examples.vi: Biết vậy thì tôi đã nghe lời mẹ. Had I

faqs.question: Can I use “đáng lẽ ra” in both spoken and written Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đáng lẽ ra” is used in both formal and informal contexts, but it’s more commonly found in reflective or narrative speech and writing to express regret or missed opportunities.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “thật tiếc là” and “đáng lẽ ra”?
faqs.answer: “Thật tiếc là” simply expresses regret or sympathy over something unfortunate, while “đáng lẽ ra” suggests that something should have happened differently, implying a sense of missed obligation or expectation.

faqs.question: How do I use “biết vậy thì” in a sentence?
faqs.answer: “Biết vậy thì…” means “If I had known, then…” and is usually followed by a statement indicating what the speaker would have done instead. It expresses regret based on hindsight.

faqs.question: Are there other common phrases to express regret in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, other expressions include giá mà (if only), ước gì (I wish), and lẽ ra (should have), all of which can express similar feelings of regret or missed chances.

faqs.question: What level of Vietnamese do I need to use these structures correctly?
faqs.answer: These expressions are generally suited for learners at the B1 (intermediate) level or above, as they require some familiarity with conditional and past narrative forms.

example.vi: Tôi hy vọng ngày mai trời sẽ nắng.

sections.heading: HOPE IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express hope in Vietnamese using common phrases like hy vọng , mong , and more. These expressions are useful in both casual and formal situations to share your wishes, expectations, or optimism about the future.

examples.vi: vọng , mong , and
examples.en: more. These expressions are useful in both casual and formal situations to share your wishes, expectations, or optimism about the future.

examples.vi: Tôi hy vọng ngày mai trời sẽ nắng. I hope it will be
Chúng tôi hy vọng dự án sẽ thành công. We hope the
vọng bạn sẽ sớm khỏe lại. I hope you will
vọng anh ấy đến đúng giờ. I hope he
Tôi hy vọng họ sẽ hiểu ý tôi. I hope they will

sections.heading: MONG = TO HOPE AND WISH
sections.explanation: “Mong” expresses a more personal wish, often with a tone of politeness. People usually use “mong” to express their needs or requests to someone politely. For.

sections.heading: CẦU MONG = TO WISH/ TO PRAY THAT…
sections.explanation: “Cầu mong” is used to express deep hope or prayers, often in situations involving strong emotions, such as worry, gratitude, or love. For.

sections.heading: ƯỚC = TO WISH
sections.explanation: “ Ước” is often used to express wishes, either realistic or fantastical. It is commonly used in reflective or imaginative contexts. For.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “hy vọng” and “mong”?
faqs.answer: “Hy vọng” is a more general and neutral way to express hope. While “mong” can sound slightly more formal or polite and is often used in expressions of wish or expectation.

faqs.question: Can “mong” be used with pronouns like “tôi” or “bạn”?
faqs.answer: Yes , “mong” can be used with personal pronouns. For example: Tôi mong bạn thành công (I hope you succeed).

faqs.question: Is there a grammatical structure I should follow when using “hy vọng”?
faqs.answer: Yes , “hy vọng” is often followed by rằng (that) or a clause, e.g., Tôi hy vọng rằng bạn sẽ đến sớm (I hope that you will come early).

faqs.question: Can “mong” be used in formal writing or letters?
faqs.answer: Absolutely . “Mong” is very common in formal or polite Vietnamese, especially in written requests or closing remarks in emails and letters, like Mong nhận được phản hồi sớm (Hope to receive your reply soon).

faqs.question: At what level should I learn to use “hy vọng” and “mong”?
faqs.answer: These expressions are suitable for A2 to B1 level learners, as they involve basic sentence structures and polite forms commonly used in everyday conversation.

example.vi: Anh ấy có thể giải quyết vấn đề này.

sections.heading: PROBABILITY IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you will learn how to express probability in Vietnamese using common phrases such as hình như , có lẽ , chắc là , and có thể. These expressions help you talk about uncertainty, possibility, and likelihood in everyday situations.

examples.vi: hình như , có lẽ , chắc là , and có thể . These
examples.en: expressions help you talk about uncertainty, possibility, and likelihood in everyday situations.

sections.heading: CÓ THỂ = MIGHT BE
sections.explanation: “Có thể” is used to express possibility or probability in the present or future. For.

examples.vi: ấy có thể giải quyết vấn đề này. He
Cô ấy có thể trở thành một bác sĩ giỏi. She

sections.heading: CHẮC LÀ = PROBABLY
sections.explanation: “Chắc là” is used to express a slight degree of certainty about an event. PATTERN Chắc là + clause For.

sections.heading: HÌNH NHƯ = APPARENTLY
sections.explanation: “Hình như” means “it seems” or “apparently,” indicating speculation based on observations or limited evidence. PATTERN Hình như + Clause For.

Hình như họ chưa biết tin này. It

sections.heading: CÓ LẼ = MAYBE / PERHAPS
sections.explanation: “Có lẽ” is used to express moderate probability or speculation. For.

faqs.question: What is the difference between “hình như” and “có lẽ”?
faqs.answer: Both express uncertainty, but hình như often implies personal perception or assumption, while có lẽ is a more neutral way to say “maybe” or “probably.”

faqs.question: Can I use “chắc là” and “có thể” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: Not exactly. Chắc là implies higher certainty (“surely”), while có thể suggests possibility without commitment (“might”).

faqs.question: Where do these expressions go in a sentence?
faqs.answer: They typically appear at the beginning or before the verb phrase: e.g., Có lẽ anh ấy chưa đến. (“Maybe he hasn’t arrived.”)

faqs.question: Are these expressions formal or informal?
faqs.answer: They are generally neutral and appropriate in both casual and polite contexts, depending on tone and situation.

faqs.question: How can I practice using these expressions naturally?
faqs.answer: Try making predictions about current events or guessing what might happen using these structures, and get feedback from native speakers or a teacher.

faqs.question: Can I go study in Vietnam with ?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can! You can study with us in Saigon or Hanoi, in both group or individual classes. You’ll even have the opportunity to live with a Vietnamese family, thanks to our homestays .

items.title: Purpose of action in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi học tiếng Anh để đi du học.

sections.heading: PURPOSE OF ACTION IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to express the purpose of an action in Vietnamese using common structures like để , nhằm , and vì. These words help explain why someone does something and are essential for forming clear, goal-oriented sentences.

examples.vi: để , nhằm , and vì . These
examples.en: words help explain why someone does something and are essential for forming clear, goal-oriented sentences.

sections.heading: ĐỂ = IN ORDER TO
sections.explanation: “Để” translates to “in order to” or “so that” and is the most common way to express purpose in Vietnamese. For.

examples.vi: Tôi học tiếng Anh để đi du học. I
Cô ấy làm việc chăm chỉ để kiếm tiền. She

sections.heading: VÌ = BECAUSE OF
sections.explanation: “Vì” is used to indicate a reason or motivation for an action. For.

sections.explanation: “Cho” is used to express actions done for the benefit or purpose of someone or something. For.

Chúng tôi tổ chức buổi tiệc cho trẻ em We

Họ xây nhà cho người nghèo They
Tôi viết thư này cho bạn bè. I

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “để” and “nhằm” when expressing purpose?
faqs.answer: “Để” is commonly used in both spoken and written language to express purpose. “Nhằm” is more formal and often appears in written or official contexts.

faqs.question: Can I use “vì” and “để” interchangeably?
faqs.answer: No. “Vì” introduces the reason or cause of an action, while “để” shows the purpose or intended result. They are not interchangeable.

faqs.question: Where in the sentence should “để” or “nhằm” be placed?
faqs.answer: They are typically placed before a verb or verb phrase to introduce the purpose, e.g., Tôi học tiếng Việt để giao tiếp tốt hơn (I study Vietnamese to communicate better).

faqs.question: Is it okay to omit “để” in casual speech?
faqs.answer: While native speakers might occasionally drop “để” in very casual or rushed speech, it’s generally better for learners to use it clearly to avoid confusion.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “vì muốn” and “để”?
faqs.answer: “Vì muốn” introduces a personal motivation ( because [I] want to… ), while “để” focuses on the intended goal of the action.

extracted_patterns: Subject + Verb + vì + Noun/ Purpose Clause

examples.en: technology has the potential to change the world

sections.heading: CÓ CƠ HỘI = TO HAVE THE OPPORTUNITY
sections.explanation: “Có cơ hội” is used to express the opportunity of someone to do something. For.

examples.vi: Tôi có cơ hội làm việc ở nước ngoài. I have the
Cô ấy có cơ hội giành chiến thắng. She has the
Họ có cơ hội hợp tác với một công ty lớn. They have the
examples.en: chance to collaborate with a big company

examples.vi: Chúng ta có cơ hội tham gia dự án quốc tế We have the
examples.en: opportunity to join an international project. Anh

Cô ấy không có cơ hội để giải thích. She
examples.en: didn’t have the opportunity to explain

faqs.question: What is the difference between “có thể” and “có khả năng”?
faqs.answer: “Có thể” is often used to express general possibility or permission, while “có khả năng” emphasizes someone’s ability or potential to do something.

faqs.question: Can I use “có cơ hội” in place of “có thể”?
faqs.answer: No, “có cơ hội” refers to having an opportunity, not just the possibility or ability to do something. It’s more context-dependent.

faqs.question: Is “có khả năng” formal or casual?
faqs.answer: “Có khả năng” is more formal and often used in written or professional contexts, while “có thể” is more common in everyday conversation.

faqs.question: How do I negate these expressions of potential?
faqs.answer: You can negate them by adding “không” in front, such as “không thể” (cannot), “không có khả năng” (lack the ability), or “không có cơ hội” (no opportunity).

faqs.question: Can these expressions be used with both verbs and nouns?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially “có thể” and “có khả năng” can be followed by verbs ( có thể đi – can go) or noun clauses ( có khả năng thành công – potential for success).

example.vi: Tuy trời mưa nhưng chúng tôi vẫn đi ra ngoài

sections.heading: EXPRESS CONTRAST IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese, expressing contrast helps you show differences between ideas, actions, or situations. This lesson will teach you how to use structures like “mặc dù” , “trong khi” , and “ngược lại” to clearly communicate opposition or comparison in your sentences.

examples.vi: “mặc dù” ,
“ngược lại” to
examples.en: clearly communicate opposition or comparison in your sentences.

sections.heading: TUY…NHƯNG = ALTHOUGH…
sections.explanation: “ Tuy… nhưng… ” translates to “ Although… ” and is used to express a contrast between two clauses. PATTERN Tuy + clause, nhưng + contrast clause For.

sections.heading: MẶC DÙ…NHƯNG = EVEN THOUGH…
sections.explanation: “ Mặc dù… nhưng… ” is similar to “ Tuy… nhưng… ” and means “ Even though… but… ” It emphasizes the unexpected result or contradiction. PATTERN Mặc dù + clause, nhưng + contrast clause For.

sections.heading: NGƯỢC LẠI = ON THE CONTRARY
sections.explanation: “ Ngược lại ” means “ on the contrary ” and is used to introduce an opposite idea or result. PATTERN Clause, ngược lại + contrast clause For.

sections.heading: TRONG KHI = WHILE
sections.explanation: “ Trong khi ” means “ while ” and is used to express contrast between two simultaneous actions or states. PATTERN Trong khi + clause, contrast clause For.

examples.vi: Tôi đang học,
bạn tôi đang chơi I am

faqs.question: Can I use “mặc dù” and “nhưng” in the same sentence?
faqs.answer: Yes . While “mặc dù” introduces the contrast, “nhưng” can be added in the second clause to emphasize it, similar to “although… but…” in English. For example: Mặc dù trời mưa, nhưng tôi vẫn đi học.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “trong khi” and “ngược lại”?
faqs.answer: “Trong khi” means “while” and is used to contrast two simultaneous actions or situations. “Ngược lại” means “on the contrary” or “in contrast” and introduces an opposite idea or result.

faqs.question: Is “mặc dù” formal or informal?
faqs.answer: “Mặc dù” is neutral and commonly used in both spoken and written Vietnamese. It is slightly more formal than “dù” but both are acceptable in most contexts.

faqs.question: Can I start a sentence with “ngược lại”?
faqs.answer: Yes . “Ngược lại” can begin a sentence to introduce a contrasting idea. Example: Tôi thích ở nhà. Ngược lại, anh ấy thích đi chơi.

faqs.question: Are there other ways to express contrast in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes. Besides “mặc dù”, “trong khi”, and “ngược lại”, you can also use “tuy nhiên” (however), “dù sao đi nữa” (anyway), and “trái lại” (conversely) depending on the tone and context.

example.vi: Tôi nghĩ bạn nên quên anh ấy đi vì anh ấy không yêu bạn đâu!

sections.heading: GIVING ADVICE IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Giving advice is a common part of everyday conversation in Vietnamese. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use expressions like khuyên , thử , and nghĩ to offer suggestions, make recommendations, and gently guide others in a natural and culturally appropriate way.

examples.vi: khuyên , thử , and nghĩ to
examples.en: offer suggestions, make recommendations, and gently guide others in a natural and culturally appropriate way.

sections.heading: TÔI NGHĨ BẠN NÊN… = I THINK YOU SHOULD…
sections.explanation: This is one of the most direct ways to offer advice in Vietnamese. It translates to “I think you should…” and is followed by the verb in its root form. This phrase is polite and widely used. For.

examples.vi: Tôi nghĩ bạn nên quên anh ấy đi vì anh ấy không yêu bạn đâu! I
examples.en: think you should forget him because he doesn’t love you!

sections.heading: BẠN THỬ…XEM SAO? = YOU SHOULD TRY…?
sections.explanation: This phrase suggests that the other person should give something a try. It is more casual and often used when offering suggestions or alternatives. For.

examples.en: tired. You should try going to a spa to relax.

sections.heading: KHUYÊN… = TO RECOMMEND / TO ADVISE
sections.explanation: This phrase is a more formal way to give advice. It is suitable for serious or professional contexts For.

examples.vi: khuyên em nên có ước mơ của riêng mình! I
examples.en: advise you to have your own dreams!

examples.vi: Họ khuyên tôi nên cưới anh ấy They

examples.vi: Tôi khuyên bạn nên từ bỏ vì chuyện tình yêu này không có tương lai I
examples.en: advise you to give up because this relationship has no future.

examples.vi: Tôi khuyên bạn nên lựa chọn chúng tôi thay vì họ I
examples.en: advise you to choose us instead of them.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “khuyên” and “góp ý”?
faqs.answer: Khuyên is used for giving advice with the intent to help or guide someone, often from a position of more experience. Góp ý is more about offering suggestions or constructive feedback, often in a neutral or collaborative tone.

faqs.question: Can I use “nên” instead of “khuyên” when giving advice?
faqs.answer: Yes. Nên is often used directly in sentences to suggest what someone should do (e.g. Bạn nên nghỉ ngơi. ). Khuyên typically introduces the act of advising (e.g. Tôi khuyên bạn nên nghỉ ngơi. ).

faqs.question: Is “thử” always used to suggest something?
faqs.answer: Not always. Thử literally means “to try.” It can be used to suggest trying something ( Bạn thử làm cái này đi. ), but can also describe an attempt ( Tôi thử học tiếng Việt. ).

faqs.question: How do I soften my advice to make it sound more polite?
faqs.answer: You can add softeners like nhé , xem sao , or use a questioning tone (e.g.

faqs.question: Bạn thử làm như vậy xem sao nhé?
faqs.answer: ) to avoid sounding too direct.

faqs.question: When should I use “nghĩ là” in giving advice?
faqs.answer: Nghĩ là (I think that…) introduces a personal opinion and is often used to gently suggest an action, especially when you don’t want to impose (e.g. Tôi nghĩ là bạn nên nói chuyện với cô ấy. ).

items.title: How to order food in Vietnamese

sections.heading: HOW TO ORDER IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: When dining in Vietnam, knowing how to order food and ask for the bill is essential for a smooth experience. In this lesson, we’ll go over the most common phrases and structures used when ordering at a restaurant in Vietnam.

sections.heading: CHO TÔI… = GIVE ME…
sections.explanation: To order something, we often start with “Cho tôi” , which means “Give me”. After that, you can mention the number , counter (like a unit for measuring items), and the name of the dish or drink. 1st pronoun (tôi, em, con, anh, chị, etc) + counter + name of the dish or drink.

sections.explanation: You might want to know how much your meal costs or ask for the bill. Here are some helpful phrases: Cái này bao nhiêu tiền? How much is it? Tính tiền! Bill, please Tôi trả bằng tiền mặt I pay in cash Tôi trả bằng chuyển khoản / thẻ I pay by bank account / card.

sections.heading: DINING IN OR TAKE-AWAY
sections.explanation: In Vietnamese restaurants, you’ll be asked whether you’re eating here or taking your food to go. Here are some common ways to express that: Ăn ở đây Eat here Mang về Take-away.

sections.heading: EXAMPLES
sections.explanation: View Dialogue 1 Customer: Chào anh! Cho tôi một bát phở bò và một ly cà phê sữa đá. (Hello! Give me one bowl of beef pho and one iced coffee with condensed milk.) Waiter: Dạ, anh muốn ăn ở đây hay mang về? (Yes, would you like to eat here or take it to go?) Customer: Ăn ở đây. (I’ll eat here.) Waiter: Vâng, anh phải đợi khoảng 10 phút. (Okay, you’ll have to wait about 10 minutes.) Customer: Cái này bao nhiêu tiền? (How much is this?) Waiter: Tổng cộng là 80.000 đồng. (The total is 80,000 VND.) Customer: Tính tiền cho tôi nhé. (Bill, please.) Waiter: Dạ, cảm ơn anh. (Okay, thank you.) View Dialogue 2 Customer: Chào cô! Cho tôi một suất cơm gà và một chai nước suối. (Hello! Give me one serving of chicken rice and one bottle of mineral water.) Vendor: Anh muốn ăn ở đây hay mang về? (Do you want to eat here or take it to go?) Customer: Mang về. (Take it to go.) Vendor: Cái này bao nhiêu tiền? (How much is this?) Customer: Tính tiền luôn nhé! (Please give me the bill!) Vendor: Dạ, anh phải đợi khoảng 5 phút nữa.

faqs.question: What is the most polite way to order food in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: The most polite way is to use “Cho tôi…” (Give me…) or “Tôi muốn…” (I would like…). You can also add “làm ơn” (please) to sound more courteous.

faqs.question: You can say: “Cho tôi xem thực đơn được không?
faqs.answer: ” which means “

faqs.question: May I see the menu?
How do I ask for the bill at a restaurant?
faqs.answer: To ask for the bill, simply say: “Tính tiền, nhé!” or more politely, “Làm ơn cho tôi xin hóa đơn.”

faqs.question: What’s the difference between “món này” and “cái này” when ordering?
faqs.answer: “Món này” refers to this dish (used specifically for food), while “cái này” means this thing in general and is less specific when talking about food.

faqs.question: Is it okay to point at the menu when ordering?
faqs.answer: Yes, it’s quite common and acceptable to politely point at the menu while saying the name of the dish or “Tôi muốn món này.”

sections.heading: ADJECTIVE CLAUSE WITH “ĐẾN MỨC” IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use “đến mức” in Vietnamese to express the extent or result of an action or quality — similar to “so… that” or “to the point that” in English. This structure helps add depth and clarity when describing intensity or consequences.

examples.vi: “đến mức” in Vietnamese to
examples.en: express the extent or result of an action or quality — similar to “so… that” or “to the point that” in English. This structure helps add depth and clarity when describing intensity or consequences.

sections.heading: ĐẾN MỨC… = SO…THAT…
sections.explanation: The phrase “ đến mức “ in Vietnamese is used to indicate the extent or degree of something. It is similar to “to the point that” or “to the extent that” in English. This structure helps add depth and emphasis when describing situations or characteristics. For.

Tôi đói bụng đến nỗi tôi ngất xỉu I was so h

faqs.question: What does “đến mức” mean in Vietnamese grammar?
faqs.answer: “Đến mức” means “to the extent that” or “so…that” and is used to express the degree or result of an action or description.

faqs.question: How is “đến mức” different from “quá… đến nỗi”?
faqs.answer: While both indicate result or extent, “quá… đến nỗi” emphasizes excessiveness, whereas “đến mức” can describe both moderate and extreme degrees more neutrally.

faqs.question: Can I use “đến mức” in both spoken and written Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes, “đến mức” is appropriate in both contexts and is commonly used in formal and informal situations to show consequence or intensity.

faqs.question: Where does “đến mức” appear in the sentence?
faqs.answer: “Đến mức” typically follows an adjective or verb phrase and precedes a clause or descriptive phrase showing the outcome or degree.

faqs.question: Are there synonyms for “đến mức” in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Yes , phrases like “tới mức” or “đến nỗi” can be used similarly, though they may differ slightly in tone or emphasis depending on context.

example.vi: Sự thành công

sections.heading: NOMINALIZATION IN VIETNAMESE
sections.explanation: Nominalization (danh từ hóa) helps transform actions and qualities into abstract nouns , making your sentences sound more natural and academic. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use nominalization correctly and effectively in Vietnamese! There are two main ways to create nominalization in Vietnamese:

examples.vi: từ hóa)
examples.en: helps transform actions and qualities into abstract nouns , making your sentences sound more natural and academic. In this lesson, you’ll learn how to use nominalization correctly and effectively in Vietnamese! There are two main ways to create nominalization in Vietnamese:

sections.heading: SỰ
sections.explanation: The word “sự” is often used to nominalize adjectives and some verbs , making them function as nouns. PATTERN Sự + Verb/Adjective → Noun For.

examples.vi: Thất bại (v) → Sự thất bại (n) To fail (v) → Failure (n) Thành công (v) → Sự thành công (n) To

sections.heading: VIỆC
sections.explanation: The word “việc” is used to nominalize specific tasks, work, or responsibilities. PATTERN Việc + Verb→ Noun (Task/Responsibility) For.

Làm (v) → Chuyện làm (n) To work (v) → Task / Job (n) Pha trà (v) → Chuyện pha trà (n) To make tea (v) → Tea
Nói chuyện → Chuyện nói chuyện (n) To talk (v) → Talking (n) Xem TV (v) → Chuyện xem TV (n) To
Viết sách (v) → Chuyện viết sách (n) To

sections.note: You can use “Chuyện” as an alternative to “Việc” when you want to refer to a “matter”, “thing”, “story” in an informal and friendly way.

sections.explanation: Chuyện học của em như thế nào? How is your studying going? Chuyện làm freelancer của em tới đâu rồi? How is your freelancing going? faqs.question: What is nominalization in Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Nominalization is the process of turning verbs or adjectives into nouns using words like sự , việc , or chuyện , allowing abstract ideas or actions to be expressed as nouns.

faqs.question: When should I use “sự” instead of “việc” or “chuyện”?
faqs.answer: Use sự for formal, abstract nouns (e.g. sự thành công – success), while việc refers more to actions or tasks ( việc học – the act of studying), and chuyện is used for events or stories, often informal.

faqs.question: Can I use “sự” before any verb?
faqs.answer: Not all verbs can be nominalized with sự . It typically applies to Sino-Vietnamese words and more formal verbs. Native speakers may not use it with purely native or colloquial words.

faqs.question: Is there a difference between “việc học” and “sự học”?
faqs.answer: Yes. Việc học emphasizes the activity of studying, while sự học is more abstract and formal, referring to the concept or process of education in a broader sense.

faqs.question: Is nominalization common in spoken Vietnamese?
faqs.answer: Việc and chuyện are quite common in everyday speech, while sự appears more frequently in written or formal contexts like news, reports, or academic texts.

items.title: Passive voice in Vietnamese

example.vi: Tôi được dắt đi bơi bởi ba tôi

sections.heading: VIETNAMESE PASSIVE VOICE
sections.explanation: Vietnamese speakers usually use a positive passive voice when they want to stay humble and show their respect to the other person or to express their honor by doing something. We guide you through the structure and usages of được and bị , helping you express actions from a recipient’s perspective.

examples.vi: được and bị ,
examples.en: helping you express actions from a recipient’s perspective.

sections.heading: AFFIRMATIVE
sections.explanation: When you want to express the action being done by someone/something in a positive way, please follow either 1 or 2 patterns below: PATTERN #1 Subject + được + Verb + bởi + someone/something.

examples.vi: dắt tôi đi bơi ôi được dắt đi bơi bởi ba tôi Tôi được ba dắt đi bơi Dad
examples.en: takes me to go swimming (1) I am taken to go swimming by dad (2) I am ( by dad) take to go swimming

examples.vi: Mẹ dạy tôi nấu ăn Tôi được dạy nấu ăn bởi mẹ tôi Tôi được mẹ dạy nấu ăn Mom
examples.en: teaches me how to cook (1) I am taught how to cook by mom (2) I am ( by mom) taught how to cook Active Passive Active Passive

examples.vi: Tôi làm bánh cho bạn trai tôi ăn Bạn trai ăn bánh được làm bởi tôi Bạn trai được tôi làm bánh cho ăn I bake a cake for my

examples.en: sister to listen (1) My sister listens to a book read by me (2) My sister has me read book for her to listen

sections.heading: NEGATIVE
sections.explanation: You just need to add “không” (means “not”) before “được” to make a negative sentence: PATTERN #1 Subject + không được + Verb + bởi + someone/something.
sections.pattern: Subject + không được + someone/something + Verb Examples:

examples.vi: không dắt tôi đi bơi Tôi không được dắt đi bơi bởi ba tôi Tôi không được ba dắt đi bơi Dad does not take me to go

examples.en: don’t read book for my sister to listen (1) My sister listens to a book not read by me (2) My sister not have me read book for her to listen
